Public information verified 2026-07-04. Company website (camcodeglobal.com), ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk), Companies House (company 07947287), company LinkedIn page.

companies.summary: UK OPSEC, COMSEC, and INFOSEC consultancy offering operational security, communications security, and information security services to high-profile individuals and private offices.
companies.body:
# Canary Zero

> UK OPSEC, COMSEC, and INFOSEC consultancy offering operational security, communications security, and information security services to high-profile individuals and private offices.

## What they do

Canary Zero provides security and privacy services across three disciplines: operational security (OPSEC), communications security (COMSEC), and information security (INFOSEC). The company's personnel have backgrounds in former UKIC and British Military operations. Clients are primarily high-profile individuals, families, and private or family offices requiring bespoke security. Services are delivered as a concierge model covering tailored threat modelling, digital footprint auditing, adversary profiling, managed digital identities, secure travel planning, and real-time threat intelligence. The company also holds a registered presence in Dubai.

## Products and solutions

- **OPSEC Services** - Digital footprint auditing, threat modelling, adversary profiling, tailored posting protocols, managed digital identities, and secure travel planning.
- **COMSEC Services** - Securing client communications against interception and unauthorised access.
- **INFOSEC Services** - Safeguarding data against compromise, alteration, or loss.

## Links

- Website: https://canaryzero.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/canary-zero

Public information verified 2026-07-04. Companies House (14664394); canaryzero.com; LinkedIn company page.

# Candy Group

> Advisory, equipment supply and secure logistics firm serving government, law-enforcement and defence customers.

## What they do

Candy Group provides advisory services, security and defence equipment supply, and secure logistics to government, law-enforcement and defence organisations. Its work covers project design, proposal and project management, compliance and ITAR-related advice, and the procurement, licensing and delivery of equipment. The company handles the transport of security-sensitive, hazardous and high-value cargoes.

companies.body:
# Capitol
> Model-agnostic, agentic AI platform that helps organisations produce decision-quality intelligence from their own data.
## What they do
Capitol AI is a Washington DC-based enterprise AI company with a London office serving UK and European customers. The platform is model-agnostic and agentic, designed to help organisations make sense of their own data and produce well-crafted documents, reports and summaries, with every output traceable to source material and each step logged for a complete audit trail. Customer sectors include defence, consulting, media, healthcare, policy and finance. The UK push, led from a London office opened in early 2026, is positioned around regulated, high-trust customers.

## Products and solutions
- **Capitol AI platform** - Agentic, model-agnostic decision-quality intelligence platform that turns structured and unstructured data into reports, briefs and other decision-grade artefacts, run inside the customer's own infrastructure with no data egress.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# Captec Group

> Designer and manufacturer of specialised, rugged and embedded computing systems for defence and other critical applications.

## What they do

Captec designs and engineers specialised computers, integrated systems and subsystems for challenging, critical and certified applications. Its platforms combine high-performance computing, electro-mechanical design and system integration for deployment in demanding operating environments. It works with OEMs and systems integrators to deliver application-specific solutions with long-term availability, configuration control and full lifecycle support.

## Products and solutions

- **Specialised computers** - application-specific industrial, compact, embedded and rackmount computers, plus custom tablets and design-to-order builds.
- **Rugged servers** - the SRS (Specialised Rugged Server) range in 1U/2U and short-depth formats, engineered against shock, vibration, EMI, temperature and unstable power; CRS-215 Rugged GPU Server.
- **Secure Encrypted Server (SES) range** - SES-120 and SES-110 with military-grade AES-256 hardware encryption and tamper-evident chassis, developed with Viasat.
- **Integrated systems** - unified multi-configuration cabinet and enclosure platforms.
- **Subsystems engineering** - metalwork, professional finishing to BR 3939 defence standards, electro-mechanical integration and build-to-print.
- **Unified Edge** - distributed edge processing and the E-Series embedded/edge/IoT range.
- **Quadbox** - hardware platform for simulation and training applications.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001 (quality); ISO 14001 (environmental); Cyber Essentials Plus.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group (Manufacturing Organisations category).

## Recent news

- **2026-04-27** - Published commentary on managing deployed defence systems amid accelerating threats. [source](https://www.captec-group.com/managing-deployed-defence-systems-amid-accelerating-threats/)
- **2026-04-09** - Published guidance on BR 3939 defence finishing standards ("warpaint"). [source](https://www.captec-group.com/its-not-just-paint-its-warpaint/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/captec

## Sources

# Carbon ThreeSixty Ltd

> Advanced composite structures for aerospace, defence, automotive and energy.

## What they do

Carbon ThreeSixty is an innovator in advanced composite structures, providing turnkey solutions across aerospace, defence, automotive and energy. The company specialises in resin transfer moulding (RTM) and novel preforming techniques such as tailored fibre placement (TFP) to produce highly optimised, cost-effective lightweight structures. It was established in 2017 and is based in Chippenham.

## Products and solutions

- **Carbon fibre wheels** - for automotive and defence applications.
- **Rotor blades** - for UAV and eVTOL vehicles.
- **PEMD components** - parts for power electronics, machines and drives applications, including electric motor components.
- **Hydrogen storage solutions** - composite storage structures.
- **TFP and RTM engineering** - design and manufacture services using tailored fibre placement and resin transfer moulding.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials Plus.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

[source](https://www.compositesworld.com/news/carbon-threesixty-supports-gkn-led-aspire-program)

Carl Zeiss Ltd is the UK operating company of ZEISS, the German optics and optoelectronics group headquartered in Oberkochen. companies.body:
# Cassi

> AI-powered probabilistic forecasting and decision support platform for defence and government.

## What they do

Cassi is a UK AI startup building a probabilistic forecasting and decision support engine for high-stakes environments. The company has a core defence and government focus: its CEO, Dr Keith Dear, is a former RAF Intelligence Officer and former Expert Adviser to the Prime Minister on Science, Technology, Space, and Modernising Defence. Cassi submitted AI forecasting evidence to Parliament's Defence Select Committee and actively engages on autonomous systems, military AI strategy, and UK defence modernisation.

## Products and solutions

- **Cassi superstrategy engine** - Probabilistic decision support platform that forecasts outcomes, quantifies risk, and recommends strategies. Benchmarked as a leading AI forecaster on ForecastBench at time of Parliamentary submission.

## Recent news

- **2025** - Dr Keith Dear gave evidence to Parliament's Defence Committee on autonomous systems and AI trajectory. [UK Defence Journal](https://ukdefencejournal.org.uk/uk-defence-warned-against-complacency-on-drones-and-ai/)
- **2025** - Cassi's AI forecaster submitted probabilistic evidence to Parliament estimating a 20% chance of Britain being drawn into a major war before 2036. [Cassi blog](https://blog.cassi-ai.com/p/our-ai-evidence-to-parliament-suggests)

## Links

# Castle Precision Engineering

> Glasgow precision machining company supplying complex components to aerospace and defence customers.

## What they do

Castle Precision Engineering is a high-performance machining supplier of complex parts and assemblies for aerospace and defence customers. It manufactures high-precision gas turbine components for the aerospace and energy sectors, and complex prismatic parts for the defence, space and medical sectors. The company runs a computerised manufacturing facility in Glasgow.

## Products and solutions

- **Aero engine components** - precision machined critical rotating parts for gas turbine engines.
- **Defence components** - machined parts associated with aircraft, radars, countermeasures, missiles, lasers and torpedoes.
- **Prismatic components** - complex prismatic parts for defence, space and medical applications.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

[source](https://www.rolls-royce.com/media/press-releases/2024/25-07-2024-rr-awards-pound-85m-multi-year-contract-to-glasgows-castle-precision-engineering.aspx)

> Designs and manufactures technical surveillance equipment for government, military and law-enforcement customers.

## What they do

Cat's Whisker Technologies designs and manufactures technical surveillance equipment for government, homeland security, military and law-enforcement agencies. Its in-house capabilities cover electronics, software and mechanical engineering, alongside UAV consultancy. The company is based in Ashtead, Surrey, and supplies customers and sales partners internationally.

## Products and solutions

- **Technical surveillance equipment** - covert and technical surveillance products for government, military and law-enforcement use.
- **Engineering services** - electronics, software and mechanical engineering.

companies.name: Catalyst Corporation Ltd
companies.summary: Defence innovation consultancy building synthetic environments and prototypes for autonomous and uncrewed systems.
companies.body:
# Catalyst Corporation Ltd

> Defence innovation consultancy building synthetic environments and prototypes for autonomous and uncrewed systems.

## What they do

Catalyst Corporation creates real-time 3D simulations and synthetic environments to explore future technology at reduced risk and cost. It supports ideation, rapid prototyping and user testing, ranging from paper and code prototyping through to working technology demonstrators in complex system simulations. The company works on land and air systems, including drones, robotic and autonomous systems and uncrewed platforms. It is based at the Sussex Innovation Centre, University of Sussex.

## Products and solutions

- **SE2 synthetic environment** - real-time 3D environment containing uncrewed air and ground vehicle models that can be controlled directly (human in the loop) or autonomously using AI and machine learning approaches.
- **Prototyping and simulation services** - low-fidelity through to advanced prototyping for defence systems development.

Catalyst has worked with Dstl, Thales and Digital Concepts Engineering on the Land Digital Robotics and Autonomous Systems Integration Capability (L-DRIC) project, and has experimented with open electronic architecture standards such as DEF STAN GVA.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Links

- Website: https://catalystcorporation.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/catalystcorporation

companies.name: CAV Systems Ltd
companies.hq: New Tredegar, Wales (group); manufacturing at Consett, England
companies.summary: Aircraft ice protection and drag reduction technology for general aviation, commercial aerospace and defence.
companies.body:
# CAV Systems Ltd

> Aircraft ice protection and drag reduction technology for general aviation, commercial aerospace and defence.

## What they do

CAV Systems designs, certifies and manufactures aircraft ice protection systems and drag reduction technology. Its products are supplied to general aviation, commercial aerospace and defence markets. The company traces its origins to 1942, when the TKS fluid ice protection method was developed for RAF aircraft, and it manufactures from a site in Consett, County Durham.

## Products and solutions

- **TKS Ice Protection System** - fluid-based anti-ice and de-ice system certified for Flight Into Known Icing, installed across many aircraft types.
- **SLD Guard** - ice protection addressing supercooled large droplet icing and associated certification requirements.
- **microLASE laser-perforated surfaces** - laser-perforated panels used for drag reduction.

CAV's technologies have been applied on defence and aerospace platforms including the C-17, Eurofighter and unmanned aircraft.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2023-09-06** - The Loar Group Inc. completed its acquisition of CAV Systems Group. [source](https://www.cav-systems.com/news/the-loar-group-inc-announces-acquisition-of-cav-systems-group/)

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cavsystems

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-04. Company website (cav-systems.com), CAV Systems LinkedIn company page, ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk), Companies House (company 10715185), Loar Group acquisition announcement. CAV Systems is part of the CAV Systems Group, which is owned by Loar Group Inc., a US-headquartered (New York) listed group; UK design and manufacturing continues from Consett, County Durham.

companies.body:
# Cavendish Nuclear Limited

> Nuclear services company supporting the UK's defence submarine fleet and civil nuclear sector, a subsidiary of Babcock International Group.

## What they do

Cavendish Nuclear is a wholly owned subsidiary of Babcock International Group and is the UK's largest supplier to the nuclear industry. It provides nuclear engineering, support, decommissioning and waste management services across defence and civil programmes. In defence, the business supports the Royal Navy's nuclear-powered submarine fleet, including through-life support and life extension across submarine classes, and provides support to the Atomic Weapons Establishment and the Submarine Delivery Agency.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence submarine support** - through-life support and life extension for the UK submarine fleet.
- **Submarine decommissioning** - defuelling, decommissioning and dismantling of legacy submarines.
- **Civil nuclear services** - engineering, decommissioning, waste management and new-build support.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# CBRNergetics Ltd

> A manufacturer and supplier of demolition stores, EOD and C-IED equipment, and CBRN detection equipment for military, government and police users.

## What they do

CBRNergetics Ltd manufactures and supplies defence equipment to military, government and police forces. Its range covers demolition stores, explosive and shaped charges, initiation and detonation systems, pyrotechnics, battle simulation, demining equipment, EOD and counter-IED solutions, and CBRN detection products. The company operates a service centre in Dorset and counts the British Ministry of Defence, the US Department of Defense, the US Department of Homeland Security, NATO forces, and United Nations bodies among its public customer base.

## Products and solutions

- **Demolition stores** - explosive charges, shaped charges, Bangalore Torpedo, sheet, tape and paste explosives, and initiation devices.
- **CBRN detection** - UK distributor for PROENGIN chemical and biological detection equipment (including AP4C) and for SERSTECH chemical, explosive, narcotic and pharmaceutical detection equipment, with calibration and servicing at its Dorset service centre.
- **EOD and C-IED** - equipment and stores for explosive ordnance disposal and counter-IED tasks.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

> An advanced composites design and manufacturing firm serving the aerospace, defence, security and space sectors.

CCP Gransden designs, develops and manufactures advanced composite components, with a focus on continuous fibre thermoplastic composites including high-performance polymers such as PEEK, PPS, PEI and polyamides. The company traces its origins to a Belfast shipbuilding and repair business and now operates from a facility in Newtownards, County Down. It serves aerospace, transportation, security and defence customers.

## What they do

CCPI Europe supplies high-accuracy temperature measurement and calibration solutions for the aerospace and defence industry. Its products include thermocouples, RTDs and reference sensors used across heat treatment, forging, composites and materials processing. The company runs a UKAS-accredited calibration laboratory and supports aerospace special-process requirements such as AMS2750 and NADCAP-aligned practices. It is based at Vector 31 Business Park in Sheffield.

# CDS Defence & Security

> A technical consultancy delivering support engineering, training, and cyber security and information assurance services to the defence and security sector.

## What they do

CDS Support Ltd, trading as CDS Defence & Security, provides support services for the delivery and through-life management of defence systems and equipment. Its work spans support engineering, technical documentation and integrated logistics support; training, learning and leadership development; and cyber security and information assurance including risk and governance, technical assurance, penetration testing, digital forensics and incident response. The company operates from secure premises in Gloucestershire and serves government, defence and prime contractor customers.

## Products and solutions

- **Support engineering** - integrated logistics support, technical documentation and through-life management of defence equipment.

# Celestica

> Global electronics manufacturing services provider with an aerospace and defence business and a UK manufacturing site at Telford.

## What they do

Celestica is a global electronics manufacturing services (EMS) company providing design, engineering, electronics manufacturing, hardware platform and supply chain services across the product lifecycle. It serves aerospace and defence, communications, enterprise and cloud, healthtech, industrial, capital equipment and smart energy markets. The business runs two segments: Advanced Technology Solutions, which contains the Aerospace and Defense unit, and Connectivity & Cloud Solutions. The UK arm trades as Celestica Limited (Companies House 03228362) and operates from the Castle Farm Campus in Telford, Shropshire; the ADS membership is held under the parent operating partnership Celestica International LP. The group is headquartered in Toronto and is listed on the New York and Toronto stock exchanges (CLS).

## Products and solutions

- **Aerospace and defence manufacturing** - low-to-medium-volume, high-mix printed circuit assembly and box-build assembly for commercial, general aviation and military electronics.
- **System integration and test** - complex system integration, test and upgrade of electronic products.
- **Maintenance, repair and overhaul** - MRO and after-market services for electronic products in aviation and defence applications.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

# Cell Pack Solutions

> UK manufacturer of custom battery packs and rugged rechargeable lithium power systems, marketed to security, emergency services and defence under the Tracer brand.

## What they do

Cell Pack Solutions designs and manufactures custom battery packs in the UK using a range of cell chemistries, supplying sectors including security and access control, emergency and medical, oceanographic and environmental monitoring, smart metering, surveillance, asset tracking and defence. Its Tracer Power brand provides rugged rechargeable lithium 12V and 24V battery systems engineered and manufactured in the UK. The company is based in South Shields and has supplied custom battery packs to the emergency services for over 25 years. In April 2024 it became part of the Celltech Group within Addtech Electrification, a unit of the Swedish listed group Addtech AB.

## Products and solutions

- **Custom battery packs** - bespoke packs across Lithium-Ion, Lithium Polymer and Lithium Iron Phosphate chemistries.
- **Tracer Power batteries** - rugged, lightweight portable rechargeable lithium 12V and 24V batteries marketed to security, emergency services and defence.
- **Chargers and testers** - battery chargers and testing equipment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# Cellebrite DI Ltd.

> Digital intelligence company providing digital forensics and investigation tools to law enforcement, government and enterprise.

## What they do

Cellebrite is a digital intelligence company founded in Israel in 1999 and headquartered in Petah Tikva. It produces tools for the collection, analysis and management of digital data, used by law enforcement, intelligence agencies, military branches, corporate security and investigations. The company is listed on NASDAQ (CLBT) and operates offices across the US, Europe, the UK, Asia Pacific and Latin America. Its largest shareholder is Sun Corporation of Japan.

## Products and solutions

- **Mobile and device forensics** - collection and extraction of data from mobile devices and other digital sources.
- **Investigation and analytics platform** - tools to analyse and manage digital evidence across investigations, extended through recent acquisitions of Corellium (Arm-based mobile virtualisation) and SCG Canada (UAV/drone forensics).

## Recent news

companies.body:
# Centre for Process Innovation

> UK technology and innovation centre accelerating development, prototyping, and scale-up of advanced materials, printable electronics, biologics, and formulation technologies, with aerospace and defence as a named served sector.

## What they do

CPI is a not-for-profit technology innovation centre and founding member of the High Value Manufacturing Catapult. It operates five technology platforms: industrial biotechnology, printable and flexible electronics, formulation, biologics, and energy storage. Aerospace and defence is an explicit market sector. The HVM Catapult signed a formal alliance with the Ministry of Defence in September 2025 under the Defence Industrial Strategy, covering advanced materials, AI, and robotics.

## Products and solutions

- **Printable and flexible electronics** - Printed sensor arrays, NFC energy-harvesting devices, and photonic components with defence, security, and aerospace applications.
- **Advanced composites and carbon fibre** - Partnership with the National Composites Centre (announced March 2026) targeting sovereign carbon fibre capability for aerospace, defence, and clean energy.
- **CPI Enterprises portfolio** - Venture arm holding stakes in defence-adjacent companies including Graphene Composites (STANAG 4569/AEP-55 certified armour) and Q5D Technologies (automated wire harness manufacturing; US Army contract).

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** HVM Catapult member (formal MOD alliance, September 2025)
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001; ISO 14001; ISO 13485:2016; MHRA Manufacturer's Licence; FDA registered

companies.body:
# Cervus Defence and Security

> Veteran-owned defence SME providing data analytics for military training, experimentation and decision support.

## What they do

Cervus is a data analytics company serving the defence and security market. Founded in 2013 by three Army veterans, it works in training analytics and experimentation, turning data from live, virtual and constructive simulation into analysis that supports force development and decision-making. The company is recognised as a veteran-owned, SC-cleared SME.

Cervus is based at Porton Science Park, Porton Down, Wiltshire, and employs around 15 people. Its work has included instrumented live fire tactical training experiments for the British Army.

## Products and solutions

- **Hive** - military training analytics platform that ingests live, virtual and constructive simulation data and presents dashboards for analysis and optimisation.
- **XCALIBR** - app-based system providing real-time marksmanship data to soldiers, commanders and instructors on weapon handling and accuracy.
- **Wargames and synthetic environments** - custom digital wargames built for experimentation, concept development and capability assessment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-09-09** - Cervus and Skyral announced a partnership to support military training and decision-making. [Skyral](https://www.skyral.com/2025/09/09/cervus-and-skyral-announce-strategic-partnership-to-transform-military-training-and-decision-making/)

# CES Advanced Composites UK

> UK arm of CES, designing and manufacturing advanced composite parts and ballistic protection for defence and aerospace platforms.

## What they do

CES Advanced Composites designs and manufactures advanced composite parts and assemblies, and ballistic protection solutions for land, naval and aerospace platforms. Services include composite tool manufacturing, structural design, material characterisation and non-destructive testing.

CES Advanced Composites UK Ltd is the UK entity of the wider CES group, whose main operations are based in Turkey. The UK facility was set up as an R&D and manufacturing site for advanced armour and composite systems, with the aim of supporting MoD and OEM programmes in-country and serving UK and European customers. The company has a facility in Coventry.

## Products and solutions

- **Ballistic armour** - helmets, vests, inserts, shields and EOD search suits for personnel protection.
- **Composite defence systems** - radar and sonar domes, land vehicle composite parts, missile parts and reflective antennas.
- **Engineering services** - composite design, tooling and testing for aerospace and defence applications.

companies.body:
# CGI Inc.

> Global IT and business consulting firm delivering systems integration and managed services to defence and government, with a large UK arm.

## What they do

CGI is an information technology and business process services firm founded in 1976 and headquartered in Montreal, Canada. It provides advisory services, software solutions, systems integration, secure managed services and training. In the UK it works with the Ministry of Defence and wider government on complex, secure mission-critical information systems spanning back-office, logistics and frontline operations, as well as space domain awareness.

## Products and solutions

- **BOREALIS** - command, control and data processing software for UK Space Domain Awareness, delivered into the National Space Operations Centre to help monitor and protect satellites.
- **Aurora** - existing UK space surveillance capability on which BOREALIS builds.
- **Systems integration and secure managed services** - advisory, software, hosting and training across defence and government programmes.

## Recent news

[Via Satellite](https://www.satellitetoday.com/government-military/2026/05/22/uk-inaugurates-new-space-domain-awareness-software-borealis/) [GOV.UK](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/new-uk-made-space-system-to-help-protect-military-satellites)

companies.name: CHAOS Industries
companies.summary: US defence-technology company building distributed radar and sensing networks that detect airborne threats faster than traditional radar.
companies.body:
# CHAOS Industries

> US defence-technology company building distributed radar and sensing networks that detect airborne threats faster than traditional radar.

## What they do

CHAOS Industries develops a multi-product portfolio of advanced sensing and detection systems powered by its Coherent Distributed Networks (CDN) technology. The products are aimed at warfighters, commercial air operators and border-protection teams, detecting and tracking drones, missiles and aircraft. The company describes itself as built by US veterans and Silicon Valley engineers, and is classified under Defense and Space Manufacturing.

## Products and solutions

- **Coherent Distributed Networks (CDN)** - flagship technology category. A self-forming network of time-synchronised sensor nodes using two-way time transfer and cooperative data processing, interoperable with command-and-control systems.
- **Vanquish (CH001)** - distributed early-warning radar; expeditionary, low size, weight and power, for early warning and tracking against UAS, missiles and aircraft.
- **Astria (CH002)** - long-range radar for high-precision tracking and extended-range surveillance.

## Recent news

- **2025-11-13** - Raised a 510m USD Series D led by Valor Equity Partners, taking total funding past 1bn USD at a 4.5bn USD valuation; also announced the acquisition of Ziva Corporation. [source](https://www.chaosinc.com/news/chaos-industries-raises-510-million-led-by-valor-equity-partners-to-accelerate-next-generation-defense-systems)
- **2025-12-16** - US Army added CHAOS Industries' radar technology to its acquisition marketplace. [source](https://www.defensedaily.com/army-adds-chaos-industries-radar-technology-to-acquisition-marketplace/unmanned-systems/)
- **2025-10-15** - Announced a partnership with Forterra on integrated autonomous counter-UAS capabilities. [source](https://www.chaosinc.com/news/chaos-industries-and-forterra-announce-partnership-to-deliver-integrated-autonomous-counter-uas-capabilities)

## Links

- Website: https://www.chaosinc.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/chaosindustries

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website, LinkedIn company page, BusinessWire, Defense Daily. Founded 2022; HQ Los Angeles, with further offices including Washington DC, San Diego, London and Amman. Headcount is a LinkedIn range (51-200); given 229 associated members and the scale of funding the firm may be approaching the large band, but the verifiable public figure places it in the medium band. Key investors include Valor Equity Partners, NEA, Accel and 8VC.

companies.body:
# Chelton

> Designer and manufacturer of antennas, avionics and land equipment for military and commercial aviation and defence platforms.

## What they do

Chelton designs and manufactures antennas, avionics and land equipment for military and commercial platforms, with a history in the sector spanning over 75 years. It operates through three business units: Antennas, Avionics and Land. Its antenna work covers tuned, low-profile and conformal airborne antennas, along with RF and digital subsystems operating from HF to microwave frequencies.

Chelton is part of TransDigm Group, the US-listed aerospace components group, following the rebrand of Cobham Aerospace Connectivity to Chelton in 2021. It is headquartered in Marlow, Buckinghamshire, with further UK sites including Redhill (Surrey) and Newmarket (Suffolk), and employs around 500 people across its locations.

## Products and solutions

- **Antennas** - tuned, low-profile and conformal airborne antennas, plus RF and digital subsystems, for civil and military aircraft.
- **Avionics** - air traffic control systems, static dischargers and communications and navigation equipment.
- **Land** - land communications and equipment, including LTE-based radio products.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

> UK manufacturer of expendable countermeasures for air and naval platforms.

## What they do

Chemring Countermeasures is the UK-based manufacturer of expendable countermeasures within the Chemring group. It develops and manufactures flares, chaff and decoys to protect military and civilian aircraft and naval vessels against guided missile threats. # Chemring Group Plc

> Listed defence technology group supplying countermeasures, energetic systems and sensors to the aerospace, defence and security markets.

## What they do

Chemring Group is a UK-headquartered defence technology business listed on the London Stock Exchange (LSE: CHG). It operates through two segments: Countermeasures and Energetics, and Sensors and Information. The group develops and manufactures expendable countermeasures, energetic materials, propellants, pyrotechnic devices, sensors and electronic systems for air, sea and land platforms.

## Products and solutions

- **Countermeasures** - expendable countermeasures including flares, chaff and decoys for air and naval platforms.
- **Energetics** - propellants, energetic materials, cartridge and propellant actuated devices, missile components and separation sub-systems.
- **Sensors and Information** - sensors and electronic systems for defence and security applications.

## Group relationship

Chemring Group Plc is the listed parent of the Chemring group of companies. UK subsidiaries include Chemring Countermeasures Limited and Chemring Energetics UK Limited.

Cherton Secure is a veteran-owned and operated company delivering security risk mitigation solutions to government, business and critical national infrastructure. Services span physical security, threat intelligence, information security and incident response, covering organisations' physical, digital and technical environments.

- **Security advisory consultancy** - strategic threat identification and assessment, geopolitical and regional risk analysis, risk monitoring and early warning, and insider threat advisory.

# Chess Dynamics

> A systems house designing and manufacturing surveillance, fire control and electro-optical defence systems for land, sea and air.

## What they do

Chess Dynamics designs and manufactures integrated defence systems and electro-optical solutions for land and maritime domains, with capability extending to air defence. Its portfolio covers surveillance, targeting and fire control systems, counter-UAS protection, and real-time video and image processing for electro-optic systems. The company is based in Horsham, with further sites in Plymouth and Wokingham, and employs over 200 people. It is part of Cohort plc, which acquired a majority stake in 2018.

## Products and solutions

- **SeaEagle** - electro-optical and fire control systems for maritime surveillance, target tracking and naval gunfire control.
- **Hawkeye** - modular multi-sensor systems for land-based reconnaissance, surveillance, targeting and ground-based air defence.
- **Counter-UAS (C-UAS)** - drone detection, tracking and defeat systems integrating optics, AI tracking and layered countermeasures.
- **Vision4ce** - AI-powered target detection, classification and tracking built on DEFT tracking and CHARM edge processing.

companies.summary: Specialist supplier and installer of GNSS, timing and synchronisation systems, including for defence and security customers.
companies.body:
# Chronos Technology

> Specialist supplier and installer of GNSS, timing and synchronisation systems, including for defence and security customers.

## What they do

Chronos Technology designs, installs and supports GNSS (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo and BeiDou) and time synchronisation systems. The company supplies simulators, repeaters, receivers, antennas and infrastructure, and works in positioning, navigation and timing (PNT). Customers span defence and security, telecom, energy, transport, broadcast and finance. Chronos has supplied and installed GNSS repeating and distribution systems for the Ministry of Defence and RAF, including for maintenance hangars, Quick Reaction Alert shelters and military vehicles. The company also works in GPS jamming detection and location.

Chronos transitioned to employee ownership through the Chronos Technology Employee Ownership Trust in 2022. It is based at Lower Lydbrook in the Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire.

## Products and solutions

- **GNSS and GPS systems** - simulators, repeaters, receivers, antennas and infrastructure for GNSS reception and distribution.
- **GNSS repeating and distribution** - indoor GNSS coverage for test facilities, maintenance hangars, QRA shelters and inside land, air and marine vehicles.
- **Timing and synchronisation** - PTP, NTP and SyncE based resilient timing for critical national infrastructure.
- **Interference detection** - GPS jamming detection and location technology.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025** - Chronos exhibited at DSEI 2025. [source](https://chronos.uk/news/meet-chronos-at-dsei-2025/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/chronos-technology-ltd

companies.body:
# Cinch Connectivity Solutions

> Manufacturer of interconnect products and cable assemblies, including mil-spec connectors for aerospace and defence.

## What they do

Cinch manufactures connectors, cable assemblies and interconnect products for harsh environment applications across aerospace, defence, industrial and communications markets. Its defence range includes rugged mil-spec connectors and cable assemblies designed, manufactured and tested for military operating conditions. The UK arm operates a fibre-optic facility near London Stansted that holds MIL-DTL-790 status, and the wider business holds FAA-PMA approvals for connectors and harnesses used in commercial and military applications.

Cinch is part of Bel Fuse Inc., a US-listed group that acquired Cinch Connectors in 2010. The Cinch business operates in the UK through two registered companies that are part of the same group: Cinch Connectivity Solutions Limited (company number 02086232), wholly owned by Cinch Connectors Limited (company number 02178707). Both are ADS members. This page covers the single Cinch business; the two entries are the same trading operation. UK manufacturing is at Worksop, England.

## Products and solutions

- **Mil-spec connectors** - rugged connectors and cable assemblies built and tested to military standards for defence platforms.
- **Fibre-optic interconnect** - Mil-Spec D38999 fibre-optic connectors produced at the UK facility holding MIL-DTL-790 status.
- **RF and microwave components** - RF connectors, microwave components and cable assemblies.
- **Specialist connectors** - D-subminiature, filtered D-subminiature, Micro-D and modular connector ranges.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** MIL-DTL-790 status (UK fibre-optic facility); FAA-PMA approvals.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2026** - Cinch exhibited in the ADS UK Pavilion at the Eurosatory defence and security exhibition in Villepinte, France. [source](https://www.cinch.com/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.cinch.com
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/showcase/cinch-connectivity-solutions/

# Citation Cyber

> UK cyber-security provider supporting the aerospace and defence supply chain with penetration testing, compliance and security awareness services.

## What they do

Citation Cyber is a UK cyber-security provider offering penetration testing, security consultancy, continuous vulnerability scanning and monitoring, compliance support and security awareness training. Its team operates from Lancaster University's Centre of Excellence in Cyber Security Research. It is a subsidiary of The Citation Group, which acquired the business (then Mitigate Cyber) in 2022.

- **Penetration testing** - NCSC-approved CHECK penetration testing for primes and tiered suppliers.
- **Compliance support** - assistance with Defence Cyber Certification (DCC), JOSCAR registration, Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus and ISO 27001.
- **Security awareness training** - phishing simulation and NCSC-certified training.

companies.name: Civica UK Limited
companies.summary: Public-sector software company whose defence work includes digital transformation and data governance for the British Army and Defence Digital.
companies.body:
# Civica UK

> Public-sector software company whose defence work includes digital transformation and data governance for the British Army and Defence Digital.

## What they do

Civica is a public-sector-focused software company supplying cloud software to government bodies across local and central government, defence, health and care, education, justice and blue-light services. Its portfolio covers workflow and automation, risk and compliance, workforce management, financial management and data analytics. The wider Civica group is owned by Blackstone, which completed its acquisition following the deal announced in late 2023.

## Products and solutions

- **Army Headquarters digital transformation** - a contract of over £5m supporting Army HQ, with architecture teams delivering to the Information Data Authority through to March 2026.
- **Defence Digital data work** - data governance, data management training, production of JSP 441 and a defence data analytics platform since 2019.
- **MoD Enterprise Data Management** - data architecture, governance and quality work under a DAMA framework.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; techUK; Armed Forces Covenant signatory.

## Recent news

- **2024-09-11** - Civica awarded a contract of over £5m supporting Army Headquarters digital transformation, with core architecture teams running to 31 March 2026. [source](https://www.civica.com/en-us/news-library/army-headquarter-civica-digital-transformation-contract/)
- **2023-11-22** - Blackstone announced the acquisition of Civica, valuing the group at just under £2bn. [source](https://www.blackstone.com/news/press/blackstone-completes-acquisition-civica/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.civica.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/civica

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Sources: company website (civica.com); ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/civica-uk-ltd-1); Companies House (find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/01628868); GOV.UK Armed Forces Covenant; Blackstone press release. Headcount figures relate to the wider Civica brand, not the single legal entity.

companies.name: Claresys Limited
companies.summary: Designer and manufacturer of covert video surveillance optics for police, military and government end users.
companies.body:
# Claresys

> Designer and manufacturer of covert video surveillance optics for police, military and government end users.

## What they do

Claresys designs and manufactures patented covert camera optics and opto-electronic systems for law enforcement, security services and armed forces. Several of its underpinning technologies were originally developed by the UK's Defence Science and Technology Laboratory (DSTL) and spun out via Ploughshare. The company is now part of the Danish-headquartered Covidence group, giving it an integrated offering across recording, transmission, control and video analytics.

## Products and solutions

- **Miniature pinhole camera lenses** - covert optics viewing through pinholes of around 0.3mm.
- **Covert pinhole PTZ lenses** - pan, tilt and zoom lenses for C-mount cameras.
- **Telephoto and long-range PTZ lenses** - compact and long-range covert surveillance optics.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Counter Terrorism Suppliers Forum (CTSF).

## Recent news

- **2025-03** - Exhibited covert surveillance optics at Security & Policing 2025, Farnborough. [source](https://www.securityandpolicing.co.uk/exhibitors/exhibitors-list-2025/claresys-ltd/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/claresys-ltd

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Sources: company website (claresys.com); ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/claresys-ltd-2); Ploughshare spin-out listing (ploughshare.co.uk); Counter Terrorism Suppliers Forum (ctsf.org.uk); Security & Policing exhibitor listings; Companies House (find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/06501929). Now part of the Danish-headquartered Covidence group, hence the owned-foreign tag.

companies.name: Clear Star Group
companies.summary: Organisational transformation and portfolio management consultancy serving UK central government and defence, founded by a team with direct MOD experience.
companies.body:
# Clear Star Group

> Organisational transformation and portfolio management consultancy serving UK central government and defence, founded by a team with direct MOD experience.

## What they do

Clear Star Group delivers lean portfolio management, operating model design, agile delivery, leadership development, and transformation programmes for UK public sector and defence clients. The founding team brings direct MOD experience: one co-founder served as Deputy Principal Staff Officer to the Chief of the Defence Staff and as Head of Defence Commitments and Crisis; another spent 20 years in Crown Service across central government and national security. The firm positions as a specialist alternative to large incumbent consultancies.

## Products and solutions

- **Lean Portfolio Management** - Structured approach to aligning investment and delivery capacity to organisational strategy.
- **Operating Model Design** - Service and organisational design for government transformation programmes.
- **Agile Delivery and Training** - ICAgile-accredited training across four certified courses; Kotter Certified Change Leadership.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (Crown Commercial Service, supplier ID 721031)
- **Accreditations:** Kotter Certified Change Leadership (one of two UK-accredited firms); ICAgile training partner
- **Memberships:** Armed Forces Covenant signatory; Defence Employer Recognition Scheme (Bronze)

## Recent news

- **2024-10-01** - Co-founder Jon Malcolm publishes commentary on empowering defence personnel to address counter-intelligence and security problems. [Clear Star Group](https://clearstargroup.co.uk/blog/solve-your-counter-intelligence-and-security-problems-by-empowering-your-people/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/clear-star-group

companies.name: ClearSpace Today Ltd
companies.summary: UK arm of a Swiss-founded space group developing in-orbit servicing and active debris removal missions.
companies.body:
# ClearSpace Today

> UK arm of a Swiss-founded space group developing in-orbit servicing and active debris removal missions.

## What they do

ClearSpace develops in-orbit servicing and active debris removal missions, covering inspection, life-extension, refuelling, disposal and repair of satellites and the capture and de-orbit of defunct objects. ClearSpace Today Ltd is the UK subsidiary of ClearSpace SA, founded in Switzerland in 2018 and headquartered in Renens; the group also operates in Germany, Luxembourg and the United States. The UK entity leads the UK Space Agency's first active debris removal mission.

## Products and solutions

- **CLEAR** - lead on the UK Space Agency's first active debris removal mission, removing two derelict objects from low-Earth orbit.
- **ClearSpace-1** - ESA-commissioned active debris removal mission targeting the PROBA-1 satellite, expected to launch around 2028.
- **PRELUDE** - ESA in-orbit demonstration mission announced in January 2026, due to launch in 2027.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.
- **Programmes:** UK Space Agency (CLEAR); ESA Space Safety Programme (ClearSpace-1, PRELUDE).

## Recent news

- **2026-01-12** - ESA and ClearSpace announced the PRELUDE in-orbit servicing and debris removal mission. [source](https://spacenews.com/esa-and-clearspace-announce-prelude-in-orbit-servicing-and-debris-removal-mission/)
- **2025-05** - ClearSpace completed the second phase of the UK CLEAR debris removal mission, de-risking its robotic capture system and testing debris detection algorithms. [source](https://spacenews.com/clearspace-completes-second-phase-of-uk-debris-removal-mission/)
- **2024-09-11** - UK Space Agency awarded a £2.35m contract to ClearSpace to accelerate the CLEAR mission. [source](https://satnews.com/2024/09/11/uk-space-agency-awards-new-contract-to-clearspace-to-accelerate-space-debris-removal-mission/)

## Links

- Website: https://clearspace.today
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/clearspace-sa

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Sources: company website (clearspace.today); ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/clearspace-today-ltd-1); UK Space Agency and ESA news; SpaceNews; SatNews; Companies House (find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/13249835). Group HQ is in Renens, Switzerland; the UK entity dates from 2021, hence the owned-foreign tag. UK-only headcount is not publicly broken out.

companies.body:
# Cloudbreak Analysis

> British provider of intelligence analysis and open-source intelligence training and consultancy.

## What they do

Cloudbreak Analysis is a British company specialising in intelligence analysis training and consultancy, delivered in the UK, online and internationally. It builds intelligence analysis capability in public and private sector organisations and delivers online investigation and analysis services. Its team holds current SC and DV security clearances.

## Products and solutions

- **Intelligence analysis training** - a portfolio of courses covering Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Social Media Intelligence (SOCMINT), Strategic Intelligence Analysis and Structured Analytical Techniques.
- **Tool training** - courses on IBM i2 Analyst's Notebook and IBM iBase.
- **Investigation and consultancy** - online investigation and intelligence analysis services delivered via secure platforms, with research available in multiple languages.

companies.name: CloudRF
companies.summary: UK provider of online radio propagation planning software for military, emergency services and communications professionals.
companies.body:
# CloudRF

> UK provider of online radio propagation planning software for military, emergency services and communications professionals.

## What they do

CloudRF offers a software-as-a-service platform and propagation API for modelling radio wave propagation. The tools support tactical radio deployment and are used by governments, industry, academia and NGOs. The software runs in a web browser and supports 1m LiDAR, soft clutter and 3D mesh terrain models. The company field tests and drive tests its software to calibrate accuracy. It is based in Gloucester, with regional servers in Canada, the UK and Germany.

## Products and solutions

- **CloudRF SaaS platform** - browser-based radio planning and coverage modelling.
- **Radio propagation API** - a 3D propagation interface for integration into other systems, applied to defence, IoT, 5G, autonomous vehicles, drones and blue-light services.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://cloudrf.com/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cloudrf

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Sources used: company website (cloudrf.com), ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk).

# CloudSecure

> UK provider of secure cloud and application engineering for defence and national security.

## What they do

CloudSecure provides cloud and DevOps engineering services to clients in demanding security environments, with a focus on secure cloud and application development for defence and national security. Its team is drawn from UK national security and defence backgrounds. The company embeds its engineers with clients and designs using secure architectural patterns. It is a signatory to the Armed Forces Covenant.

companies.body:
# Clover Group

> Specialist freight forwarder and logistics provider serving the defence, aerospace, security and space sectors.

## What they do

Clover Group, founded in 1993 as The Clover Shipping Company Ltd, provides international logistics, customs compliance and supply chain services to regulated sectors including defence, aerospace, security and space. Operations span the UK, US and EU. The UK office runs with security cleared personnel and proprietary digital systems. The group operates through subsidiaries covering global logistics, warehousing and distribution, managed services, consultancy and IT services.

- **Clover Global Logistics** - international freight forwarding and customs compliance.
- **Clover Distribution** - warehousing and distribution.

companies.summary: West Midlands engineering manufacturer producing components for defence, off-highway and automotive customers.

# Clydesdale Engineering

> West Midlands engineering manufacturer producing components for defence, off-highway and automotive customers.

## What they do

Clydesdale Engineering manufactures componentry for defence, off-highway and automotive customers, from pressings to complex assemblies and fabrications. It operates from a West Midlands facility and employs around 240 people across its divisions, which include Defence Dynamics, Accles & Pollock and Clydesdale Jones, supplying defence, aerospace, automotive and off-highway sectors.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence Dynamics** - anti-vibration and shock isolation mounting solutions for military, marine, aerospace and commercial applications, including a naval mountings range used by the UK MoD and other navies, with shock mounts that reduce explosive shock forces to protect critical equipment.
- **Accles & Pollock** - precision tube and engineered products.

companies.summary: Northern Ireland electronic contract manufacturer providing PCB assembly and product build for sectors including defence and aerospace.
companies.body:
# CMASS NI

> Northern Ireland electronic contract manufacturer providing PCB assembly and product build for sectors including defence and aerospace.

## What they do

CMASS is an electronic contract manufacturer based near Antrim in the Greater Belfast area. It provides electronic manufacturing services including printed circuit board assembly (PCBA), complete product build, assembly and testing, configure-to-order, integration, product upgrade, refurbishment and rework. The company describes serving customers across telecommunications, power, defence, aerospace and connected health.

CMASS was founded in 2010 by former Nortel and Avaya manufacturing staff and operates from an 18,500 square foot factory off the M2 motorway. It carries out high-complexity, low-volume assembly work alongside customised functional, systems and network testing.

## Products and solutions

- **Printed circuit board assembly (PCBA)** - electronic assembly for low-volume, high-complexity production.
- **Product build and integration** - complete product assembly, configure-to-order, integration, upgrade, refurbishment and rework.
- **Test services** - customised functional, systems and network testing.

companies.name: CNC Tool & Die Limited
companies.summary: Lisburn precision machining business producing components to aerospace and defence standards.
companies.body:
# CNC Tool & Die

> Lisburn precision machining business producing components to aerospace and defence standards.

## What they do

CNC Tool & Die is a precision CNC machining business in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, part of the Coggan Group. It provides machined components to aerospace, defence and space, automotive, motorsport, pharmaceutical and medical device customers. The company runs 3- and 5-axis CNC machining centres with automation and focuses on small to medium batch work, typically up to quantities of around 50, with rapid response, new product introduction and prototyping.

For aerospace and defence it produces components such as gears, housings, sensor mounts, brackets, manifold blocks, structural panels, fittings and connectors. The business was founded in 2005 as a sister company to Coggan Engineering Ltd.

## Products and solutions

- **Precision CNC machining** - 3- and 5-axis machining of complex components to tight tolerances.
- **Aerospace and defence components** - gears, housings, sensor mounts, brackets, manifold blocks, structural panels, fittings and connectors.
- **Prototyping and NPI** - rapid response, new product introduction and prototype machining.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS9100 Rev D (held since 2022), the aerospace and defence quality management standard.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

companies.summary: Portland-based designer and manufacturer of electronic and electromechanical systems and test solutions for defence, aerospace and subsea, working as a subcontractor to UK prime defence contractors.
companies.body:
# CodaOctopus Martech

> Portland-based designer and manufacturer of electronic and electromechanical systems and test solutions for defence, aerospace and subsea, working as a subcontractor to UK prime defence contractors.

## What they do

Coda Octopus Martech is a designer and manufacturer of electronic and electromechanical systems and special test solutions for defence, aerospace, subsea and science applications. It is based at Osprey Quay, Portland, Dorset. Its business model is the supply of sub-assemblies into larger mission-critical programmes as a subcontractor to prime defence contractors, with scope typically running from concept and design through prototype, manufacture and post-sale support.

The company has operated as a subcontractor to UK and European prime defence contractors since 1988 and holds pre-qualifications used to take part in major defence contract programmes. It is part of Coda Octopus Group, Inc, a US company listed on Nasdaq.

## Products and solutions

- **Bespoke electronic and electromechanical systems** - custom sub-contract design and manufacture of high-integrity devices in small quantities.
- **Special test solutions** - test equipment for high-end defence and subsea applications.
- **Defence sub-assemblies** - sub-components supplied into mission-critical defence programmes run by prime contractors.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

[GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/04/24/3067334/0/en/Coda-Octopus-Group-Reports-1-4m-Engineering-Services-Order.html)

companies.name: Cogent Technology
companies.summary: UK contract electronics manufacturer serving military and defence, aerospace, medical, marine, and industrial customers from its Felixstowe facility.
companies.body:
# Cogent Technology

> UK contract electronics manufacturer serving military and defence, aerospace, medical, marine, and industrial customers from its Felixstowe facility.

## What they do

Cogent Technology is a contract electronics manufacturer (CEM) with four decades of operating history. It provides PCB assembly, electromechanical assembly, box build, conformal coating, and new product introduction (NPI) through to series production. Military and defence is one of five named specialist verticals. Cogent was acquired by CamdenBoss in January 2024, joining the Heathpatch Group, and continues to operate independently from its Felixstowe site.

## Products and solutions

- **PCB assembly** - Through-hole, surface mount, and mixed-technology assembly to IPC-A-610 standards, including conformal coating for harsh environments.
- **Box build and electromechanical assembly** - Full device build from sub-assembly through to finished product.
- **NPI and series production** - New product introduction support from prototype to volume manufacture.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001; ISO 13485; ISO 14001; FDA registered

## Recent news

- **2025-11-03** - Cogent acquires the commercial EV charging hardware platform and manufacturing business from EO Charging, expanding its GreenTech vertical. [East Anglian Daily Times](https://www.eadt.co.uk/news/25628415.cogent-technology-suffolk-buys-eo-charging-platform/)
- **2024-01-01** - Cogent Technology acquired by CamdenBoss, joining the Heathpatch Group electronics manufacturing group. [CamdenBoss](https://www.camdenboss.com/news/posts/2024/january/camdenboss-welcomes-cogent-technology-to-the-heathpatch-group/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cogent-technology

companies.name: Cohere UK, Ltd.
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of the Canadian enterprise AI company Cohere, building secure foundation models and AI products for business and government.
companies.body:
# Cohere UK

> UK subsidiary of the Canadian enterprise AI company Cohere, building secure foundation models and AI products for business and government.

## What they do

Cohere is a security-first enterprise AI company that builds foundation models and end-to-end AI products for business and government. Its platform offers private deployment so organisations can keep data behind their own firewalls, deployable across major clouds, private cloud, on-premises, or air-gapped environments. The London office supports the company's R&D growth and its sovereign-AI work for government.

## Products and solutions

- **Command** - family of large language models for text generation.
- **Embed and Rerank** - search and retrieval models.
- **North** - agentic AI platform launched August 2025 for private, on-premises, hybrid-cloud, VPC, or air-gapped deployment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.name: Cohort plc
companies.summary: AIM-listed parent group of independent UK and European defence technology businesses spanning land, maritime and air markets.
companies.body:
# Cohort

> AIM-listed parent group of independent UK and European defence technology businesses spanning land, maritime and air markets.

## What they do

Cohort is the AIM-listed parent of a group of defence technology businesses providing services and products to UK, German, Portuguese, Australian and international customers in defence and related markets. The group model combines the responsiveness of smaller independent businesses with the stability and market access of a listed group. It reports in two segments: Communications and Intelligence, and Sensors and Effectors.

## Products and solutions

- **Chess Dynamics** - surveillance, tracking and fire-control systems for defence and security.
- **EID** - communications systems for naval and military customers (Portugal).
- **ELAC SONAR** - sonar systems and underwater communications for naval customers (Germany).
- **EM Solutions** - satellite communications terminals for navy and maritime customers (Australia); acquired January 2025.
- **MASS** - data technology for defence and security, focused on electronic warfare, digital services and training support.
- **MCL (Marlborough Communications)** - electronic and surveillance technology for UK end users including the MOD.
- **SEA** - technology-based products for defence and transport markets, plus research and training services.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** Listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM market (ticker CHRT); complies with AIM Rule 26 and benchmarks governance against the QCA code.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2026-05** - Cohort group showcased its naval capabilities at the Combined Naval Event, 19 to 21 May 2026. [source](https://www.cohortplc.com/news/press-release/cohort-group-showcase-latest-naval-capabilities-combined-naval-event)
- **2025-01-31** - Cohort completed the acquisition of EM Solutions, an Australian satellite communications business, for an enterprise value of around 75 million pounds. [source](https://www.cohortplc.com/news/press-release/cohort-plc-acquires-em-solutions)

## Links

- Website: https://www.cohortplc.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cohort

## Sources

The ADS member entry is out of date; the Cohort website is the current source for the seven-business structure and around 1,600 staff across four countries. Subset tagging additionally drew on the Chess Dynamics, MASS and EM Solutions pages of cohortplc.com for surveillance, fire-control, EW/CEMA, training and satcom detail.

companies.name: Collaboraite Ltd
companies.summary: Secure, user-centred data and AI products for research, investigation and data analysis, built for law enforcement, government and defence customers.
companies.body:
# Collaboraite Ltd

> Secure, user-centred data and AI products for research, investigation and data analysis, built for law enforcement, government and defence customers.

## What they do

Collaboraite designs and builds secure, user-centred products that use data, analytics and AI to address operational challenges, working through design thinking and agile delivery. Its data and AI toolset, licensed as the Collaboraite capability and accessed through the Collab.hq interface, is built to accelerate research, investigation and data analysis, with a stated focus on internet intelligence and investigation (i3), open-source intelligence (OSINT), and consented or open public data. Collaboraite is part of the Investigo Group, alongside Vestigo Consulting Ltd and Internet Investigation Solutions (IIS), whose Long Arm platform provides the secure hosting environment through which the Collaboraite capability is delivered. The company is a member of the D3IP community at Defence BattleLab, Dorset Innovation Park.

## Products and solutions

- **Collaboraite capability** - data and AI products and engines, including data science, machine learning, deep learning and natural language processing (NLP), plus automation and robotic process automation (RPA), accessed via the Collab.hq interface.
- **Data and AI solutions consultancy** - digital, data and analytics discovery and transformation strategy; data fusion and migration via API; data lake, warehousing and virtualisation; data visualisation through licensed (Power BI) and open analytics tools; secure-by-design, GDS-compliant application and product build.
- **Agile and design thinking consultancy** - agile coaching, design thinking and agile transformation training and implementation support, listed separately on G-Cloud.
- Solutions are hosted in UK Police Assured Secure Facilities (PASF) private data centres with disaster recovery provision.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (Digital Marketplace) - "Collaboraite Data and AI Solutions" and "Collaboraite Agile & Design Thinking Training and Consultancy" listings.
- **Memberships:** D3IP community, Defence BattleLab, Dorset Innovation Park.
- **Partnerships:** Strategic partnership with ShadowDragon, announced March 2024, to bring ShadowDragon's OSINT software and investigative tools to shared customers through the Collaboraite platform, alongside joint training delivered with Vestigo.

## Recent news

- **2024-03** - ShadowDragon entered a strategic partnership with Collaboraite and Vestigo Consulting to build trust in OSINT, giving shared customers direct, secure access to ShadowDragon's OSINT tools through the Collaboraite platform. [ShadowDragon](https://shadowdragon.io/blog/shadowdragon-enters-strategic-partnership-with-collaboraite-and-vestigo-to-build-trust-in-osint/)

# Com Dev Europe

> UK space subsystem manufacturer within Honeywell Aerospace, making space-qualified microwave and RF hardware for satellite payloads.

## What they do

Com Dev Europe designs and manufactures space-qualified hardware: passive microwave equipment, active RF and electronic equipment, ferrite devices, and integrated assemblies for communications, Earth observation and scientific satellites. Its UK sites at Aylesbury and Edinburgh deliver these payload subsystems. The company is part of Honeywell Aerospace following Honeywell's acquisition of COM DEV International in 2016.

## Products and solutions

- **Passive microwave equipment** - integrated multiplexers, filters and ancillary microwave components.
- **Active RF and electronic equipment** for satellite payloads.
- **Ferrite devices and electromechanical RF switches.**
- **Integrated assemblies** for communications, Earth observation and scientific satellite payloads.

# Comand AI

> AI-native command-and-control and decision-support platform for defence operations.

## What they do

Comand AI is a Paris-based deep-tech defence software company, founded in 2023, with a presence in London. It develops AI-powered capabilities for command and control of defence and security operations, with outputs designed to be transparent, traceable and contestable, and with a human making every final decision.

## Products and solutions

- **Prevail** - an AI-native command-and-control (C2) platform for planning and decision-making under dynamic, multi-domain conditions. It processes complex battlefield data and simulates courses of action quickly, and provides post-operation analysis tools.

CoMech Metrology provides verification, calibration and precision measurement services from custom-built laboratories in Derby. Services can be delivered by sending equipment to the laboratories or by on-site engineer visits, and the company supplies asset management software for calibration compliance.

companies.name: Commerce Decisions Limited
companies.summary: Provider of tender evaluation software and procurement support services for major defence and public procurements.
companies.body:
# Commerce Decisions

> Provider of tender evaluation software and procurement support services for major defence and public procurements.

## What they do

Commerce Decisions supplies software and expert services for the evaluation of complex, high-value tenders. It has worked with the UK Ministry of Defence since 2001, and its AWARD evaluation tool is integrated with the MOD Defence Sourcing Portal for the assessment of high-risk or complex bids. Its services team supports buyers from planning through to contract award, including sensitivity analysis, risk and cost scenarios, negotiation support and competitive dialogue.

Commerce Decisions was formerly part of QinetiQ Group. It was acquired in November 2020 by N. Harris Computer Corporation, part of the Canadian-headquartered Constellation Software, so the company is foreign-owned while operating from the UK.

## Products and solutions

- **AWARD** - tender evaluation software for strategic and complex procurements, integrated with the MOD Defence Sourcing Portal.
- **Expert services** - procurement planning, evaluation design, sensitivity analysis, negotiation and debriefing support.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (AWARD specialist services listed on the Digital Marketplace).

> A British designer and manufacturer of high-performance embedded computer products for critical defence and industrial applications.

## What they do

Concurrent Technologies Plc develops and manufactures system-orientated solutions and high-performance computer products, based primarily on Intel processor technology, for use in critical embedded applications. Its products are used by integrators across the defence, security, aerospace, telecommunications, transportation, medical and industrial markets. The company is based in Colchester and is listed on the London Stock Exchange under the ticker CNC.

companies.summary: Digital identity and access management specialist working across government, defence and the wider public and private sectors.
companies.body:
# Condatis Group

> Digital identity and access management specialist working across government, defence and the wider public and private sectors.

## What they do

Condatis designs and implements identity and access management solutions, with a focus on Microsoft Entra and a Zero Trust approach to authentication and credential verification. It works in decentralised identity and verifiable credentials, and is a member of the Decentralized Identity Foundation and the Open Identity Exchange. Condatis has delivered identity projects across UK government and the public sector, including customer identity work with Defra and a verifiable-credentials staff passport proof of value with Sellafield and Digital Catapult.

The business demerged from Sitekit Group in 2022 and was acquired by Bridgepoint in October 2024.

## Products and solutions

- **Identity and access management** - bespoke IAM delivery built on Microsoft Entra, including Zero Trust authentication.
- **Microsoft Entra Verified ID** - verifiable credentials for workforce onboarding and help-desk identity verification.
- **Decentralised identity solutions** - verifiable-credential systems such as the Sellafield Nuclear Staff Passport proof of value.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Decentralized Identity Foundation; Open Identity Exchange. Microsoft partner.

## Recent news

- **2024-10** - Bridgepoint invested in Condatis. [source](https://www.bridgepointgroup.com/about-us/news-and-insights/press-releases/2024/bridgepoint-invests-in-digital-identity-specialist-condatis)
- **2025-07** - Condatis published work on Microsoft Entra Verified ID for secure workforce onboarding. [source](https://condatis.com/news/blog/reimagining-workforce-onboarding/)

- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/condatis

companies.name: Conducttr
companies.summary: Crisis exercise and information-operations simulation software for defence, security and corporate users.
companies.body:
# Conducttr

> Crisis exercise and information-operations simulation software for defence, security and corporate users.

## What they do

Conducttr provides crisis exercising software that creates a high-fidelity, realistic exercising environment for in-person or remote sessions. The platform models a simulated information environment of social media and news, and models target-audience reactions, with a particular focus on training for influence and information warfare. The company began in transmedia storytelling for entertainment and moved into immersive training for defence and security following Defence and Security Accelerator (DASA) funding. The legal entity is Transmedia Storyteller Ltd.

## Products and solutions

- **Crisis simulation platform** - software for building and running crisis and information-warfare exercises, with a Scenario Editor and ready-made content.
- **Live Sim** - facilitated live exercise simulation delivered on the platform.
- **Exercise-building service** - a service to design and build bespoke exercises for clients.
- **Kaliningrad Crucible** - a public wargame co-created with author and consultant Mark Galeotti, in which teams compete for control of the information space in a geopolitical crisis scenario.

## Recent news

- **2025-09** - Conducttr exhibited at DSEI 2025, ExCeL London (9 to 12 September 2025). [source](https://www.conducttr.com/dsei25)

- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/conducttr

companies.name: Conflict Armament Research Ltd.
companies.summary: Investigative organisation that documents and traces conventional weapons, ammunition and military materiel found in conflict zones.
companies.body:
# Conflict Armament Research

> Investigative organisation that documents and traces conventional weapons, ammunition and military materiel found in conflict zones.

## What they do

Conflict Armament Research tracks the supply of conventional weapons, ammunition and related materiel into conflict-affected areas. Field teams document weapons at the point of use, often working with national security and defence forces, then trace items back through the chain of supply to identify how they reached the battlefield. The organisation has documented over 130,000 illicit weapons, munitions and related items across multiple countries since 2014. It has received funding from the European Union and the German, Swiss and UK governments.

## Products and solutions

- **iTrace** - a global weapon reporting system and database combining field investigations with weapon-tracking records; the public reporting interface is free to use.
- **Field investigation and tracing** - in-conflict documentation of weapons and supply-chain analysis for governments and policymakers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025-09** - Conflict Armament Research documented surface-to-air missiles recovered in Sudan, identifying manufacture in China and Bulgaria. [source](https://sudanreeves.org/2025/09/29/news-compendium-september-29-2025/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.conflictarm.com/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/conflict-armament-research

companies.body:
# Conjay Collectors Ammunition Co. Limited

> Defence supplier of armour and ballistics ammunition, explosives and narcotics test kits, and counter-IED equipment, trading in the sector since 1954.

## What they do

Conjay has traded within the defence sector since 1954, initially supplying ammunition to the British MOD and police, then to armour manufacturers and ballistics test ranges. The company supplies explosive test kits, munitions for ballistics and armour trials across international test standards, and counter-IED equipment, and operates a design bureau producing bespoke machines for ballistics research. The wider Conjay group has expanded into security technologies including counter-UAV, secure communications and optics.

## Products and solutions

- **Armour and ballistics ammunition** - munitions supplied for armour and ballistics trials against international test standards including STANAG and NIJ.
- **Explosive test kits** - EXPEN, EXPRAY and DROPEX + kits for confirmation of explosives traces.
- **Narcotic test kits** - drug detection kits.
- **Counter-IED equipment** - equipment and project support for counter-terrorism requirements.
- **Conjay Design Bureau** - bespoke machines for ballistics research, including an automatic bullet puller.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, Cyber Essentials
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## What they do

Connor Solutions is an electronic manufacturing services (EMS) provider taking customer designs through to manufactured product. It offers design for manufacture and new product introduction, supply chain management, PCBA and final assembly manufacturing, test and inspection, and aftermarket support. Defence and aerospace is one of its named market sectors, alongside medical and life sciences, energy and industrial, space, and interconnectivity. The company operates a 112,000 square foot facility and can produce complex multilayer RF PCBA and high-level electromechanical assemblies.
