# Exsel Electronics

> Software-centric systems integrator focused on data-centric security and multi-domain information management for defence and government.

## What they do

Exsel Electronics is a division of the Exsel Group, operating as a standalone legal entity based in Welshpool, Powys. It is a software-centric systems integrator with a specialised focus on information and cyber security, working closely with governments and defence organisations to pioneer interoperable information security systems. Its core domain covers multi-domain communications, data, and system management, with an emphasis on ensuring users have continuous security and accessibility of information across all domains. The approach aligns with the UK National Cyber Security Strategy.

## Products and solutions

- **Need2Know** - a document-level data protection product that safeguards secret, sensitive, or personal information contained within documents before they are shared, providing a single source of truth for information control.
- **Data Centric Security** - a unified security model for governments and organisations that protects data and ensures business continuity across hybrid work environments, cloud computing, and complex regulatory frameworks.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials
- **Memberships:** ADS Group (membership held by parent Exsel Group Ltd)

# Exsel Group

> Independent defence technology group providing engineering design and integration, electronics, training and simulation, ballistic protection, and field services to defence and industry.

## What they do

The Exsel Group is an independent defence technology solutions company delivering engineering solutions, technology systems, and support services to defence and industry where reliability, safety, and protection are paramount. Originating in 1947, the group now operates across three UK sites and serves a global customer base spanning government, defence, and commercial interests. Exsel Group is the official sole representative and approved agent for ND Defense's complete range of vehicles and parts across the UK, Europe, and West Africa.

## Products and solutions

- **Design and integration** - electromechanical, electronic, and communication systems integration into vehicle and mobile platforms; container-based solutions for rapid deployment and severe environment applications; build-to-print and full design-and-development programmes.
- **Electronics** - software and electronic engineering for defence systems including obsolescence management, through-life support, repair and maintenance, and data-centric security (delivered through the Exsel Electronics division).
- **Training and simulation** - design, production, assembly, and installation of training and simulation hardware devices for high-fidelity representative training environments.
- **Dyna Armour** - ballistic and blast protection solutions for personnel, platforms, and assets, designed and developed in partnership with Dynasystems.
- **Field services** - global maintenance, servicing, and parts supply for defence vehicle fleets including ND Defense legacy and current vehicles.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.body:
# ExTrac

> British AI-driven open-source intelligence and geopolitical risk-analytics company serving defence, security and government.

## What they do

ExTrac is a British OSINT and geopolitical risk-intelligence company. It combines curated data, human subject-matter expertise and domain-specific AI to detect and forecast risk across the physical world, online, and where the two intersect. It ingests high-relevance, hard-to-reach data streams to track violence, map narratives and surface emerging threats, supporting customers in defence, security, government, public safety, executive protection and risk monitoring. The registered entity is ExTrac AI Limited.

## Products and solutions

- **ExTrac platform** - a customisable web application for leaders, decision-makers and analysts, hiding complexity by default while keeping the underlying data fully accessible and auditable.
- **Co-Analyst** - a domain-specific, agentic AI that retrieves data, surfaces insight and generates visualisations and reports in response to analyst requests.
- **Curated data feeds and bespoke reporting** - expert-curated, hard-to-access data streams, with the option for customers to integrate their own data, plus a mobile app and bespoke reporting.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** listed on the UK G-Cloud Digital Marketplace as a supplier.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

companies.body:
# ExTrac AI

> British OSINT company providing an AI-powered intelligence platform for defence and security customers tracking conflict, grey zone, and influence activities.

## What they do

ExTrac AI Limited is a British Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) company serving allied nations. It provides both an AI SaaS platform and bespoke intelligence reporting, sourcing, cleaning, and structuring hard-to-access adversary data to deliver expert insight at machine speed. The platform finds, curates, and ingests high-relevance data sources including communications and kinetic behaviours of hostile state and non-state actors, mining them for real-time insights on conflict, grey zone, and influence activities using AI that is both secure and compliant by design. Defence and security applications include force protection, situational awareness, operational planning, and Information Environment and Battle Damage Assessment.

## Products and solutions

- **ExTrac platform (web and mobile)** - AI SaaS platform combining curated OSINT data, domain-specific AI, and human expertise; accessible via web app, mobile app, and API, with configurable dashboards, alerts, and reporting.
- **Co-Analyst** - domain-specific agentic AI built into the ExTrac platform that retrieves, analyses, and contextualises data on instruction, producing visualisations and reports.
- **Bespoke intelligence reporting** - customer-specific reporting products drawing on ExTrac's curated data combined with client data for tailored analytical output.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR registered
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/extracrisk

# F C Lane Electronics

> West Sussex specialist distributor and value-added assembler of military-grade electrical connectors and interconnect solutions.

## What they do

F C Lane Electronics is a franchised value-added distributor of military and aerospace-specification electrical connectors, established in 1966 and operating from Slinfold in West Sussex. The company holds in-house assembly and testing approval for MIL-DTL-38999 Series connectors under a QPL Category C authorisation from Eaton/Souriau, and its sister company Weald Electronics manufactures connectors to military standards on the same site. FC Lane is a BAE Systems Aircraft Business Units approved supplier (BAE/AG/20198/MAA) and holds a NATO NCAGE code (U0125). The Lodge Group umbrella covers FC Lane, Weald Electronics and Lane Motorsport.

## Products and solutions

- **Military circular connectors** - MIL-DTL-38999 Series I and III, MIL-DTL-26482, MIL-DTL-5015 and MIL-DTL-55302 connectors held in stock and available with full traceability.
- **RF and coaxial connectors and cable assemblies** - HUBER+SUHNER range for military communications and data link applications.
- **Fibre optic connectors and assemblies** - for defence and security performance-critical applications.
- **Custom connector assemblies** - in-house assembly and testing of military circular connectors to MIL-STD-790; specials design and manufacture through Weald Electronics.
- **Backshells and accessories** - circular connector backshells and accessories to AS85049.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (current 2026 certificate)
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2015 (FM588537); EN 9120:2018 / AS9120A (RS00100, distribution of electrical connectors); CECC/IECQ (distribution D2000, specialist contractor P4000, final assembly E003); QPL Category C (Eaton/Souriau approval, MIL-DTL-38999 Series I and III, micro38999, 8STA, JVS); Cyber Essentials (IASME-CE-040852); BAE Systems approved supplier (BAE/AG/20198/MAA SC4); NATO NCAGE U0125

companies.body:
# Faculty

> London applied-AI company building and deploying AI systems for defence, national security and law enforcement; acquired by Accenture in March 2026.

## What they do

Faculty is a UK applied artificial intelligence company that designs, builds and deploys AI systems for customers across defence, national security, government and commercial sectors. In defence and security it builds bespoke AI algorithms and customised off-the-shelf solutions spanning sensor enhancement, data exploitation and multi-domain command and control, with a stated emphasis on AI safety and ethical deployment. The company works with the UK Ministry of Defence, UK Strategic Command and national security and law enforcement partners. Faculty was acquired by Accenture, with the transaction completing 16 March 2026; it now operates as part of Accenture.

## Products and solutions

- **Cognitive Intelligent Sensing (CoInS)** - machine learning algorithms to autonomously process sensor data and orient sensing systems in real time; in joint development with Leonardo for unmanned platforms and ISR.
- **Electronic warfare AI** - AI capability acceleration for EW applications, developed in collaboration with Leonardo.
- **Defence AI systems** - bespoke and customised AI solutions spanning sensor enhancement, data exploitation and multi-domain command and control.
- **National security and law enforcement** - AI systems designed, built and deployed for operational use with UK security partners.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (Digital Marketplace supplier ID 703150)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.name: Faltec Europe Limited
companies.summary: Sunderland precision plastics manufacturer supplying ground systems, aerospace defence and specialist equipment programmes; UK subsidiary of Tokyo-listed Faltec Co. Ltd.
companies.body:
# Faltec Europe

> Sunderland precision plastics manufacturer supplying ground systems, aerospace defence and specialist equipment programmes; UK subsidiary of Tokyo-listed Faltec Co. Ltd.

## What they do

Faltec Europe is a UK precision manufacturer based at the International Advanced Manufacturing Park (IAMP) in Sunderland, producing injection-moulded, co-extruded and assembled components for automotive, defence and aerospace customers. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Faltec Co. Ltd, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and headquartered in Kawasaki, Japan. Its primary business is automotive Tier 1 supply, but it operates a dedicated defence sector offering covering ground systems, aerospace defence components and specialist mission equipment. Faltec Europe is JOSCAR-registered, holds ADS Group and Make UK Defence memberships, and has signed the Armed Forces Covenant. The company employs approximately 450 people at its Sunderland site.

## Products and solutions

- **Ground systems components** - internal and external vehicle system parts, structural and housing components, and lightweight shielding for military ground platforms.
- **Aerospace defence components** - structural and interior elements for defence aviation applications.
- **Specialist mission equipment parts** - bespoke injection-moulded components manufactured to customer specification for mission-specific requirements.
- **In-house manufacturing capabilities** - injection moulding (350 to 2,300 tonne presses), co-extrusion, assembly and automation, surface treatment, toolroom operations and precision testing.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (registered)
- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials; IATF 16949 (automotive quality, encompasses ISO 9001, BSI certified); ISO 14001 (LRQA certified); ISO 45001; Armed Forces Covenant signatory; FSQS registered
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Make UK Defence; NE RDSC (North East Reserve and Defence Support Community)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/falteceurope

companies.name: Far Field Exploits Limited
companies.summary: British RF engineering company designing low-profile antennas and resilient timing for the dismounted operator.
companies.body:
# Far Field Exploits

> British RF engineering company designing low-profile antennas and resilient timing for the dismounted operator.

## What they do

Far Field Exploits is a defence engineering company focused on enhancing tactical communications for the dismounted operator and the wider military and law enforcement community. It designs, develops and manufactures novel low-profile antennas for body-worn and vehicle applications, with RF engineering and microelectronics expertise that also supports research and development for government and industry partners. It is a veteran-owned business. Note: despite the name, this is an antenna and tactical-communications manufacturer, not a cyber or vulnerability-research firm; "far field" is an antenna-engineering term.

## Products and solutions

- **Sigma antenna range** - wideband, low-profile flexible antennas in tactical and covert body-worn variants, built on a patented Sigma antenna architecture.
- **Tachron** - precision wireless time and frequency transfer system providing GNSS-free synchronisation to within a few nanoseconds, resilient to jamming.
- **RF and antenna development services** - antenna design, testing and contract research and development for government and industry.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Programmes:** Completed a DASA Rapid Impact project (Tempus Wave); earlier jamming-resilience trials with Dstl.

## Recent news

- **2024-02-26** - Completed the DASA Rapid Impact project "Tempus Wave", integrating the Tachron timing system with surrogate electronic surveillance payloads for a field trial. [source](https://www.farfieldx.com/news-1/dasa-rampd-project-completed)

## Links

- Website: https://www.farfieldx.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/farfield-exploits-ltd

companies.body:
# Farsound Aviation

> Specialist distributor and supply chain solutions provider for the aero engine MRO market.

## What they do

Farsound Aviation is a UK-headquartered procurement and supply chain company serving the aero engine maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) sector. It sources, manages and distributes high-volume consumable hardware components for engine overhaul programmes, including kitting, direct line feed and vending provision. The company operates a purpose-built 58,000 sq ft automated warehouse in Brentwood, Essex, with AI-driven robotic picking. It serves MRO organisations globally, some of which support military aviation programmes.

## Products and solutions

- **Consumable hardware supply** - procurement and distribution of high-volume aero engine hardware components for MRO operations.
- **Kitting and line-feed services** - pre-kitted component sets and direct-to-line delivery for engine overhaul facilities.
- **Inventory management** - vendor-managed inventory and vending solutions for MRO customers.
- **Global distribution network** - facilities in the UK, USA (San Antonio), Spain (Madrid), Canada, Singapore and Japan.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS/EN 9120B (aerospace distributor quality management); ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001; UK CAA AC00-56 (stores organisation approval); ASA-100 (Aviation Suppliers Association)

companies.body:
# FarX

> Early-stage biometric AI company, based in the Malvern cyber cluster, developing fused voice and face recognition for continuous multi-factor identity authentication.

## What they do

FarX (trading as FARx, legally FarX Group Limited) is a Great Malvern-based company founded in September 2022 by a team of voice and face biometric specialists. It builds a proprietary machine learning technology that fuses speaker, speech and face recognition to deliver continuous, frictionless multi-factor biometric authentication of a user. FARx 1, released October 2023, is described by the company as the first fused voice-face ("talking face") recognition technology. The company states its applications include banking and financial services, retail, healthcare, education, gaming, online conferencing, and government citizen and employee authentication. FARx is delivered as an on-premises server solution or as a cloud service, accessed via API.

## Products and solutions

- **FARx 1** - fused voice, speech and face recognition engine providing continuous multi-factor biometric identity verification.
- **Sector applications** - packaged use cases for banking, retail, healthcare, education, gaming and government/citizen authentication, plus embedded use in chatbots, help desks and online conferencing.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Programmes:** Digital Catapult Machine Intelligence Garage, Cohort 17 (2022).

## Recent news

- **2023-10-19** - Released FARx 1, described by the company as the world's first fused voice-face recognition technology. [source](https://www.farx.co.uk/2023/10/17/farx-releases-farx-1-the-worlds-first-fused-voice-face-recognition-technology/)

# Fat Potato

> Defence-sector software consultancy specialising in cloud-native development, AWS migration and secure-by-design engineering.

## What they do

Fat Potato is a Cheltenham-based software consultancy that has operated primarily in the defence sector since its founding in 2016. The company delivers bespoke cloud-native software development, legacy system migration to AWS, and secure-by-design engineering for government and defence clients. It is an AWS Partner and is approved on the MOD's Neutral Vendor Framework for Innovation (Constellia NVFi). The company also develops an in-house HR platform product, Chinchill.HR.

## Products and solutions

companies.name: FAUN Trackway Limited
companies.summary: Designer and manufacturer of portable aluminium Trackway ground stabilisation systems for military and specialist civil applications.
companies.body:
# FAUN Trackway

> Designer and manufacturer of portable aluminium Trackway ground stabilisation systems for military and specialist civil applications.

## What they do

FAUN Trackway is a Welsh manufacturer of portable, recoverable Trackway panel systems used by military engineers worldwide to provide rapid access across soft ground, mud, marsh, snow and sand. Originating in wartime aluminium fabrication on the Isle of Anglesey from 1940, the company has supplied the UK MOD since the 1960s and exports to numerous NATO and partner-nation armed forces. It operates three divisions: Trackway Defence, Trackway Access (civil) and Trackway Engineering. The company is part of the German KIRCHHOFF Group (FAUN KIRCHHOFF GRUPPE). A US subsidiary, FAUN Trackway USA Inc., is based in Washington DC.

## Products and solutions

- **Military Trackway panels** - aluminium alloy portable roadway panels rated to MLC 30, MLC 60, MLC 70 and MLC 150; recoverable and redeployable.
- **Heavy Ground Mobility System (HGMS)** - vehicle-mounted rapid deployment and recovery system for high-load Trackway.
- **Medium Ground Mobility System (MGMS)** - vehicle-mounted system for medium-load Trackway deployment.
- **Airfield Damage Repair mats** - aluminium matting for rapid airfield surface repair.
- **Helicopter landing systems** - Containerised Helipad ("Helipad in a Box") and UAV and aircraft landing pads.
- **Over-the-shore access matting** - marine ingress and egress systems.
- **Engineering services** - bespoke manufacturing for defence and nuclear sectors.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001:2015; ISO 45001:2018; BS EN 1090 Execution Class 4 (structural aluminium fabrication); Fit For Nuclear (F4N, granted 2018); Investors in People
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-04** - Signed the Armed Forces Covenant with RAF Valley (Station Commander Group Captain Currie OBE). [source](https://fauntrackway.co.uk/latest-news/2025/faun-trackway-limited-signs-the-armed-covenant)
- **2023** - Signed a multi-million-pound contract with the Dutch Ministry of Defence to supply Heavy Ground Mobility Systems to the Royal Netherlands Army; final delivery running into 2025. [source](https://fauntrackway.co.uk/latest-news/2023/faun-trackway-limited-signs-multi-million-pound-contract-for-dutch-ministry-of-defence)

## Links

- Website: https://fauntrackway.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/faun-trackway

companies.name: FCDO Services
companies.summary: UK government trading fund providing technical security, secure IT, secure speech, logistics and translation services for diplomacy and defence, at home and overseas.
companies.body:
# FCDO Services
> UK government trading fund providing technical security, secure IT, secure speech, logistics and translation services for diplomacy and defence, at home and overseas.

## What they do

FCDO Services is a trading fund and arm's-length body of the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, delivering secure services to protect UK government people, data and assets worldwide. It traces its origins to secure communications work for British embassies from the 1930s, became an executive agency (FCO Services) in 2006, moved to trading fund status in 2008, and took its current name in September 2020 when the FCO and DFID merged. FCDO Services operates in more than 250 destinations across 168 countries and reported revenue exceeding £252 million in 2025/26. Customers are primarily other UK government departments, alongside foreign governments and international organisations closely linked to the UK. The organisation employed around 797 people as of February 2026.

## Products and solutions

- **UK NACE** - the UK National Authority for Counter-Eavesdropping, the National Technical Authority for technical security, providing threat identification, policy and advice, and operational technical security support to UK government, the armed forces, law enforcement and critical national infrastructure.
- **Digital and cloud services** - secure IT, digital and cloud solutions for government and partner customers.
- **Securing buildings and spaces** - technical, building and perimeter security for diplomatic and government premises overseas.
- **Secure speech** - secure speech environments protecting sensitive discussions from technical and physical eavesdropping.
- **Logistics** - global logistics, freight and deployment support for government and partner customers, run through the NetCourier logistics management system.
- **Translation and interpreting** - language services supporting UK government engagement overseas.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-09-22** - NetCourier, the new logistics management system replacing the legacy CMACS platform, went live, introducing real-time tracking, automated processes and 24-hour accessibility for government and partner customers worldwide. [source](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/fcdo-services-annual-report-and-accounts-2025-to-2026/fcdo-services-annual-report-and-accounts-2025-to-2026)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fcdo-services/

companies.body:
# FEL Avionics

> Specialist stockist, repairer and support provider for legacy defence radar systems, holding over 16,300 registered NATO/NSN part numbers.

## What they do

FEL Avionics is a subsidiary of UKC Group, operating as a stockist and distributor of discontinued piece parts and line-replaceable units for legacy defence radar systems. The company provides repair and overhaul services, onsite fault diagnosis, technician training, and a travelling wave tube test facility. Where components are globally unavailable, the team reverse-engineers and manufactures small batches of critical parts.

## Products and solutions

- **Legacy radar spares** - stock supply of discontinued components and LRUs for BAE/Plessey Radar Systems (S511, Martello, Watchman, AR-51, AR325, AR327), naval radar (994, 996), ground radar (AR3D), and US Army radars AN/TPS-70 and AN/TPS-43; also GEC Marconi, Racal Decca, and Marine Avionics lines.
- **Repair and overhaul** - fault-finding, repair, and full overhaul by technicians trained at Plessey, BAE, Marconi, and AMS.
- **TWT test facility** - Travelling Wave Tube testing capability for radar sub-assembly verification.
- **Reverse engineering and batch manufacture** - small-batch manufacture of globally unavailable critical components.
- **Lease and lend programme** - partnership plan for specialised sub-assemblies to support continuity of operations.
- **Technician training** - courses at all levels for covered radar systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.fel-avionics.com/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fel-avionics-ltd

companies.name: Fenix Insight Ltd
companies.summary: Open-source intelligence company specialising in munitions, weapons systems, and global conflict event data, serving defence, government, and humanitarian customers.
companies.body:
# Fenix Insight

> Open-source intelligence company specialising in munitions, weapons systems, and global conflict event data, serving defence, government, and humanitarian customers.

## What they do

Fenix Insight combines explosive ordnance expertise with AI and data analytics to produce structured intelligence on munitions, weapons platforms, and conflict events. Founded in 2011, the company serves UK and international civil and military government departments, humanitarian organisations, and commercial risk management customers. In addition to its data products, the company delivers specialist consultancy, capability development, incident investigation, and evidence-based policy design.

## Products and solutions

- **METIS** (Munitions, Events and Technical Information System) - the company's primary OSINT platform, launched in 2015 and rebranded to METIS in 2025. Covers over 470,000 geospatial conflict events, 6,700 technical munition, platform, and CBRN records, and 45,000 images. Delivered via secure web interface or API.
- **IRIS** (Image Recognition Identification System) - machine vision software that detects landmines and explosive ordnance from UAV video feeds in real time. Field-trialled with Ukrainian Army Engineers and the Ukrainian Ministry of Emergencies in January 2024.
- **Intelligence briefings** - monthly SITREPs, munitions newsletters, and CBRN bulletins.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, Cyber Essentials Plus
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

# Fenner Precision Polymers

> Manufacturer of engineered polymer products including aerospace-grade ducting and coated fabrics, operating as a Michelin Group subsidiary with its UK manufacturing base in Lincoln.

## What they do

Fenner Precision Polymers manufactures engineered polymer components across three global product families: industrial motion (timing and power transmission belts), high-tech coated fabrics, and air and fluid handling products. The Lincoln, UK site holds AS9100 Rev D accreditation for aerospace and defence manufacturing, covering ducting under the James Dawson brand and specialist coated fabrics and textiles under the Critical Thermal Composites brand. Fenner joined ADS Group in August 2025 to target growth in the aerospace, defence, and space markets. The company is wholly owned by Michelin Group, which acquired Fenner plc in May 2018.

## Products and solutions

- **Aerospace ducting** (James Dawson brand) - air and fluid handling products for aerospace and defence applications, manufactured at Lincoln under AS9100 Rev D.
- **Critical thermal composites** (Critical Thermal Composites brand) - specialist coated fabrics and textiles for thermal management in aerospace and defence environments, manufactured at Lincoln under AS9100 Rev D.
- **Industrial motion products** (Fenner Precision brand) - timing belts and power transmission components for wider industrial markets.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.name: Ferchau Limited
companies.summary: German-headquartered engineering services group delivering specialist aerospace and defence engineering from its UK base at Bournemouth Airport; Airbus Preferred Supplier for engineering and customer services.
companies.body:
# Ferchau

> German-headquartered engineering services group delivering specialist aerospace and defence engineering from its UK base at Bournemouth Airport; Airbus Preferred Supplier for engineering and customer services.

## What they do

Ferchau Limited is the UK entity of FERCHAU GmbH, a privately held German engineering services group founded in 1966. The UK operation, incorporated in January 2011, is based at Aviation Park West, Bournemouth Airport, with a second site in Bristol. It delivers specialist engineering services to the aerospace and defence supply chain, including structural analysis, systems design (fuel, hydraulics, and electrical systems), vibration and operational stability analyses, aerospace certification support, and embedded engineering and testing. The group's Aviation Group subsidiary RST Rostock manufactures harness test systems and cabin simulators for military platforms including Eurofighter. FERCHAU is not listed as an ADS Group member.

## Products and solutions

- **Structural and systems engineering** - stress calculations, design verification, systems design for aerospace and defence applications, delivered from Bournemouth and Bristol.
- **Certification support** - specialist assistance through the Airbus and wider aerospace regulatory certification process.
- **Embedded engineering and test** - embedded development and testing capability for aerospace and defence programmes.

companies.summary: London deep-tech R&D company developing advanced composite materials and adhesive systems for military aviation, aerospace and space applications.
companies.body:
# FAC Technology

> London deep-tech R&D company developing advanced composite materials and adhesive systems for military aviation, aerospace and space applications.

## What they do

FAC Technology (trading name of Fergusson's Advanced Composite Technology Limited) is a specialist research and development company working at the molecular level of advanced composite materials. Founded by Dr Alexander Fergusson, an aerospace composites researcher from Imperial College London, where he holds Honorary Lecturer and Academic Visitor status, the company develops high-performance composite materials, structural adhesives and potting compounds with documented application in civil and military aviation, satellite manufacturing and space systems. FAC Technology holds Civil Aviation Authority Alternative Design Organisation approval (UK.AP1002) and has achieved ETSO C90d certification for its composite air-cargo pallet. The company participated in the Advanced Propulsion Centre Technology Developer Accelerator Programme (TDAP), raising technology readiness from TRL 3 to TRL 8.

## Products and solutions

- **Deep Air Carbon (DAC) composite materials** - structural composite material system in use in civil and military aviation applications; forms the basis of the ETSO C90d-approved composite air-cargo pallet (PMC type).
- **Fire-resistant composites** - flame penetration resistant materials with a burn rate of 0 mm/min, relevant to aircraft and military vehicle applications.
- **Advanced structural adhesives** - developed for satellite manufacturing, confirmed for use in critical space applications with a leading satellite manufacturer.
- **Motor resins** - high-thermal-performance materials for electric motor applications including UAV and propulsion systems.
- **Potting compounds** - specialist encapsulation compounds for aerospace and space applications.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** CAA Alternative Design Organisation (UK.AP1002); ETSO C90d product approval (composite air-cargo pallet); Composites UK member
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fac-technology

# Fieldfisher

> International law firm advising the defence, aerospace and space sectors, with active programmes across F-35, satellite, and defence technology.

## What they do

Fieldfisher LLP is a multinational law firm providing legal services across commercial, regulatory, transactional, and technology matters. Its headline sectors include technology, financial services, energy and natural resources, and life sciences. Within those it runs specialist practices in satellite and space, international trade and sanctions, intellectual property, defence supply chain, and real estate. The firm is an ADS Group member and has a documented track record advising defence primes, MOD joint ventures, and national-security-focused space companies.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence programme legal advisory** - commercial, supply chain, IP, real estate, health and safety and regulatory advice on major defence programmes, including the F-35 global maintenance, repair, overhaul and upgrade programme for the BAE Systems / Northrop Grumman / MOD joint venture Sealand Support Services Limited.
- **Space and satellite law** - advising on satellite launches, regulatory matters, and national security space projects; acted for SatVu on the HotSat-2 launch (SpaceX Transporter-16, March 2026), a satellite carrying mid-wave infrared sensors for national security applications.
- **International trade and sanctions** - regulatory advice relevant to defence exports, dual-use goods, and Russia sanctions.
- **Defence technology procurement analysis** - published analysis on MOD's Defence AI Centre (DAIC) AI Model Arena and technology procurement processes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# Fife College

> Scottish further and higher education college delivering defence workforce development and advanced manufacturing training, with a campus embedded in Babcock's Rosyth Dockyard.

## What they do

Fife College is a Scottish further and higher education institution offering over 400 courses across engineering, construction, digital technologies, health and social care, business, and creative industries. It serves more than 20,000 students annually and runs over 420 employer partnerships. The college's Rosyth Engineering Campus sits inside Babcock International's Rosyth Dockyard on the Firth of Forth, with approximately 10,000 sq m of purpose-built training facilities focused on engineering, renewables, digital, and advanced manufacturing. The college is one of six partners in the Arrol-Gibb Innovation Campus (AGIC) at Rosyth, alongside the Universities of Edinburgh and Strathclyde, Scottish Enterprise, Skills Development Scotland, and Fife Council.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence workforce development** - bespoke programmes supplying upskilled STEM workers for the Type 31 frigate programme and other national and international defence programmes at Rosyth; includes Production Support Operative (PSO) and Trainee Welder pathways delivered under the Babcock MoU.
- **Advanced manufacturing and engineering training** - apprenticeships and programmes covering composites, robotics, nuclear manufacturing, and digital shipbuilding at the Rosyth campus.
- **Arrol-Gibb Innovation Campus** - collaborative research and training campus at Rosyth supporting the Type 31 build across advanced manufacturing, digital shipbuilding, and related disciplines.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-03** - Fife College and Forth Valley College signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Babcock International Group during Scottish Apprenticeship Week, formalising a talent pipeline for the Type 31 frigate programme at Rosyth. UK Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry Luke Pollard MP attended. [source](https://www.babcockinternational.com/news/babcock-launches-joint-skills-mou-with-fife-and-forth-valley-colleges-to-boost-scotlands-defence-workforce/)

companies.body:
# Filtronic

> AIM-listed designer and manufacturer of RF, microwave, and millimetre-wave components and subsystems for defence, space, and telecoms.

## What they do

Filtronic is a UK public company that designs and manufactures high-performance radio frequency, microwave, and millimetre-wave components and subsystems. It serves the defence and aerospace, space, telecoms infrastructure, and critical communications markets. Core capabilities include RF design, solid-state power amplifiers (SSPAs), transmit/receive modules, hybrid manufacturing, GaAs and GaN semiconductor assembly and test, plastic encapsulation of compound semiconductor devices, and electromechanical manufacturing. Principal sites are at NETPark Sedgefield in County Durham; additional UK design offices are in Leeds, Manchester, and Cambridge. The company also operates a manufacturing site in Salisbury, Maryland, USA. It is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM market under the ticker FTC.

## Products and solutions

- **Transmit/receive modules** - radar T/R modules for defence customers; the company passed 60,000 units in production as of 2025.
- **Solid-state power amplifiers** - GaN-based SSPAs for space (including E-Band SSPAs for SpaceX Starlink) and defence applications.
- **Electronic sensor system components** - bespoke RF assemblies for defence prime contractors; a £13.4 million contract for an electronic sensor system was announced in July 2025.
- **Compound semiconductor packaging** - plastic encapsulation of GaAs and GaN devices for high-density System-in-Package designs; DASA DTEP award for low-cost, low-SWAP packaging for future radar applications.
- **Satellite communications RF assemblies** - RF assemblies for low-earth-orbit satellite constellations including Airbus LEO programmes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001 (UK and USA operations); ISO 14001 (Sedgefield sites); IASME Governance Gold
- **Programmes:** DASA Defence Technology Exploitation Programme (DTEP) award recipient
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-02** - Interim results for the six months to November 2025: revenue £25.3 million; record order book; active programmes with Leonardo, QinetiQ, and BAE Maritime Systems confirmed. [source](https://filtronic.com/investors/)
- **2025-08** - £47.3 million contract from SpaceX for next-generation GaN E-Band SSPAs for the Starlink constellation; described at the time as the largest contract in the company's history. [source](https://filtronic.com/news-events/news/filtronic-secures-record-breaking-spacex-order/)
- **2025-07** - £13.4 million contract to supply components for an electronic sensor system for a European defence prime. [source](https://bdaily.co.uk/articles/2025/07/14/filtronic-secures-134-million-defence-deal)

companies.name: First Light Fusion Ltd
companies.summary: Oxford University spinout commercialising hypervelocity amplifier technology for defence, space, and fusion research customers.
companies.body:
# First Light Fusion

> Oxford University spinout commercialising hypervelocity amplifier technology for defence, space, and fusion research customers.

## What they do

First Light Fusion developed inertial confinement fusion technology using projectile-based and pulsed-power approaches. Following a strategic pivot in 2025, the company now operates two commercial lines: licensing its amplifier technology to other fusion ventures, and selling and deploying its experimental infrastructure to defence, space, and research customers. Their VIPER velocity amplifier and supporting facilities are explicitly positioned for warhead assurance, armour and materials qualification, and micro-meteoroid and orbital debris testing.

## Products and solutions

- **VIPER velocity amplifier** - a device that boosts projectile velocity beyond 10 km/s; first commercial sale delivered to Texas A&M HVIL in June 2026. Stated applications include hypervelocity impact testing, orbital debris simulation, and warhead assurance.
- **Two-stage gas gun** - the UK's largest, used for hypervelocity launch and impact testing.
- **Pulsed power facility** - Europe's largest pulsed power facility, used for high-energy physics, propulsion research, and extreme-environment testing relevant to defence materials science.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (registered January 2026)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; UKspace (start-up member)

## Recent news

- **2026-06-01** - First experimental campaign with VIPER completed at Texas A&M HVIL; hypervelocity impacts above 12 km/s achieved. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-04-01** - £25m funding round first close led by Starmaker One and East X Ventures; strategic investment from UKAEA. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-02-01** - AI collaboration with Locai Labs announced alongside tritium breeding performance validation. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-01-01** - JOSCAR registration confirmed. [ADS Group](https://www.adsgroup.org.uk)
- **2025-08-01** - World record pressure set on two-stage gas gun. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)

# Fischer Connectors

> Swiss-headquartered manufacturer of high-performance electrical and fibre-optic connectors for military, aerospace, and industrial applications; UK entity based in Waterlooville, Hampshire.

## What they do

Fischer Connectors designs, manufactures, and distributes electrical connectors, fibre-optic connectors, cable assemblies, and connectivity systems for demanding environments. Defence is a primary vertical: products are tested to MIL-SPEC and IEC standards and are used across dismounted soldier, ground vehicle, aerospace, and marine applications. The company operates under the parent group brand Conextivity Group, with the Swiss parent Fischer Connectors SA as the global headquarters. The UK entity (Fischer Connectors Ltd, incorporated 1988) handles sales and distribution for the British market.

## Products and solutions

- **KEYSTONE tactical hub** - a connectivity platform built for the Internet of Battlefield Things (IoBT), with NATO STANAG compatibility and Nett Warrior integration.
- **MiniMax 06** - an ultra-miniature connector targeting handheld defence devices and UAVs.
- **SS Series and SSC Series** - short-plug miniature connectors for high pin-density, space-saving military and instrumentation use (launched January 2026).
- **Fibre-optic connectors and cable assemblies** - for harsh-environment military communications and sensor systems.

companies.body:
# Fivecast UK

> AI-powered open-source intelligence platform for national security, defence, and law enforcement missions.

## What they do

Fivecast is an Australian-founded digital intelligence company that develops AI-enabled OSINT software for government and enterprise customers across national security, defence, law enforcement, border security, and financial crime prevention. The company was founded in 2017 from collaboration between Australian government agencies and research institutions, and has expanded into the United Kingdom and United States. Fivecast UK Ltd is the UK legal entity (Companies House 13861067), serving UK and European customers from a London-based team as part of the company's Five Eyes market expansion.

## Products and solutions

- **Fivecast ONYX** - OSINT collection and analysis platform for broad discovery, targeted data collection, and near real-time insights across millions of online sources.
- **Fivecast LUNEX** - analyst-curated dataset product that surfaces hidden threat networks and delivers rapid intelligence, integrated into the ONYX platform.
- **Fivecast MATRIX** - large-scale risk assessment solution for security vetting and screening, developed in conjunction with the US Government.
- **Fivecast Discovery** - automated digital footprinting, identity resolution, and information verification capability integrated within ONYX.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Foreign-headquartered (Adelaide, Australia) with a UK legal entity (Fivecast UK Ltd, Companies House 13861067) and a London-based team. Also operates in Washington DC, USA.

# Flann Microwave

> Designer and manufacturer of RF, microwave and millimetre-wave components and precision test instrumentation.

## What they do

Flann Microwave is a UK manufacturer of precision passive RF, microwave and millimetre-wave components, sub-systems and calibration instrumentation, operating from around 300 MHz to 1.1 THz. Trading since 1956 and based in Bodmin, Cornwall, it serves aerospace and defence, space and satellite communications, metrology and test, telecommunications, and research markets, with the majority of production exported. companies.body:
# Flare Bright

> Machine learning and digital twin software for GNSS-denied drone navigation, testing, and evaluation.

## What they do

Flare Bright develops machine learning software and digital twin technology for unmanned aerial and undersea vehicles. Founded in November 2015 and based at Westcott Venture Park in Buckinghamshire, the company builds software that enables drones to fly accurately in GNSS-denied environments, perform in-flight wind sensing, and support regulatory safety case assurance. Flare Bright has shifted from developing its own drone hardware to providing software that integrates with existing and future platforms built by defence primes and other manufacturers. It holds contracts with the UK MOD, Dstl, and the US Department of Defense.

## Products and solutions

- **Digital twin platform** - software environment that models unmanned aircraft and applies machine learning to solve navigation, control, sensing, and optimisation problems, enabling GNSS-denied flight.
- **Extera** - containment assurance software for critical drone operations, designed to accelerate testing and evaluation programmes for novel drone systems; introduced at DSEI 2025.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Recipient of multiple DASA Rapid Innovation contracts for GNSS-denied flight capability development. Fourth-sprint project with the US Department of Defense at time of most recent reporting.

## Recent news

- **2023-12-00** - Flare Bright's digital twin software entered trials with the US Department of Defense in Spain using a CATUAV drone in GNSS-denied conditions. [source](https://www.govconexec.com/2023/12/flare-brights-digital-twin-software-currently-undergoing-tests-in-us-spain/)

## Links

# Fleetwind

> ISO 9001-accredited naval systems engineering consultancy specialising in platform integration, safety assurance, and in-service support for complex warship programmes.

## What they do

Fleetwind Ltd is a maritime systems engineering company formed in 2019 and registered in Polruan, Cornwall. The company works directly with the Royal Navy, UK MOD, and defence primes across the supply chain, with its business predominantly in the defence sector. Fleetwind specialises in the integration and sustainment of complex naval platforms, covering the transition of ships and systems from the build environment into service. The team is drawn from ex-Naval personnel, business leaders, and specialist engineers with experience on high-profile programmes.

## Products and solutions

- **Platform integration and assurance** - systems engineering support for the transition of naval platforms and systems into the in-service environment, including Type 26, Type 31, and QE-Class programmes.
- **Safety case and certification** - delivery of Safety Case Reports, Naval Authority Certification submissions, and supporting assurance across Ordnance, Munitions and Explosives (OME), aviation safety, and integrated platform assurance for surface and sub-surface environments.
- **Ship-Air Integration** - development of Interface Control Documents, Ship Aviation Safety Case Reports, and Ship Air Release recommendations, supporting Naval Authority certification submissions.
- **In-service support** - integrated logistic support (ILS) disciplines, in-service support solution development, and business management advice.

companies.name: FlexSR Limited
companies.summary: University of Oxford spinout developing flexible, small-model speech recognition for defence, security and policing applications.
companies.body:
# FlexSR

> University of Oxford spinout developing flexible, small-model speech recognition for defence, security and policing applications.

## What they do

FlexSR Limited is a private company incorporated in October 2023, a spinout from the University of Oxford's Language and Brain Laboratory. Its speech recognition approach is built on a linguistic model of phonological features rather than the acoustic or large-language-model methods used by conventional automatic speech recognition (ASR), so it can recognise words and phrases across languages, accents and pronunciations without model training. The company states this lightweight approach suits real-time, low-compute deployment on edge devices, wearables, body-worn cameras or radios, including hostile or hands-free operational environments where conventional interfaces are impractical.

## Products and solutions

- **FlexSR speech recognition engine** - flexible ASR technology matching speech directly to phonological features and a defined lexicon, without acoustic model training, licensed for integration into third-party products and platforms.
- **Real-time transcription quality checker** - error-detection capability for AI-generated call transcripts and summaries, piloted with a UK police service to improve confidence in AI call-handling pipelines.
- **Hands-free trigger-word monitoring** - real-time speech monitoring for front-line police and security personnel to initiate assistance broadcasts when hands are not free to operate a device.
- **Surveillance and word-spotting** - recognition of target words and phrases directly from audio across multiple languages and accents without building per-language or per-accent models.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Other:** Worldwide patents granted (WIPO) for the underlying automatic speech recognition and speech analysis inventions. University of Oxford retains a shareholding.

## Recent news

- **2026 (event March 2027)** - Exhibiting at Security and Policing, the UK Home Office-sponsored event for police and security technology, presenting its real-time error checker for AI call-reporting pipelines. [source](https://www.securityandpolicing.co.uk/exhibitors/exhibitors-list-2026/flexsr-limited/)

## Links

- Website: https://flexsr.app
- Companies House: https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/15225314

companies.name: FLIR Systems Limited
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR, designing and manufacturing thermal imaging, surveillance sensors, and uncrewed systems for defence, security, and commercial customers.
companies.body:
# FLIR Systems

> UK subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR, designing and manufacturing thermal imaging, surveillance sensors, and uncrewed systems for defence, security, and commercial customers.

## What they do

FLIR Systems Limited is the UK-registered subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR LLC, the defence and sensing division of Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE: TDY). The UK entity was incorporated in 1977 and operates from sites in Kent and Chelmsford. FLIR supplies thermography, night vision, and surveillance equipment to military, law enforcement, and commercial customers, with a defence portfolio spanning thermal imaging for vehicles, aircraft, and dismounted soldiers, autonomous uncrewed aerial systems, and chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosives (CBRNE) detection. Teledyne completed its acquisition of the parent company, FLIR Systems Inc., in May 2021 for approximately 8.2 billion USD.

## Products and solutions

- **ThermoVision SA90-HD** - upgraded situational awareness vehicle vision system launched at Eurosatory 2026, providing HD thermal imaging for military vehicles.
- **ThermoVision DV55-HD** - HD driver vision system for military vehicles, announced alongside the SA90-HD in June 2026.
- **MilSight LIRC III** - compact vehicle-mounted weapon sight, announced at Eurosatory 2026.
- **SkyRaider and SkyRanger UAS** - tactical uncrewed aerial systems for reconnaissance and persistent surveillance.
- **SkyCarrier** - autonomous UAS launch and recovery platform for deployment from vehicles, vessels, or fixed sites, introduced at DSEI 2025.
- **Black Recon** - autonomous micro-drone system for vehicle-based reconnaissance, weighing under 450 grams; launched June 2026.
- **Handheld and airborne sensors** - thermal imaging and visible-spectrum sensor systems for soldier, maritime, and airborne applications.
- **CBRNE detectors** - chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosives detection systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Foreign-headquartered (Wilsonville, Oregon, USA). UK entity FLIR Systems Limited (Companies House 01320288) incorporated 6 July 1977. Registered office: 2 Kings Hill Avenue, Kings Hill, West Malling, Kent. UK correspondence: 106 Waterhouse Lane, Chelmsford. Parent company Teledyne Technologies listed on NYSE.

## Recent news

- **2026-06-15** - Teledyne FLIR Defense launched Black Recon autonomous micro-drone system and unveiled three upgraded vehicle vision systems at Eurosatory 2026. [source](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260615122823/en/Teledyne-FLIR-Defense-Unveils-Three-Vehicle-Vision-Systems-for-Enhanced-Battlefield-Awareness)
- **2025-09-00** - Teledyne FLIR unveiled the SkyCarrier autonomous UAS launch and recovery platform at DSEI in London. [source](https://defense.flir.com/about/news/teledyne-flir-defense-unveils-skycarrier-autonomous-uas-launch-and-recovery-platform-at-dsei-in-london/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/teledyne-flir

companies.body:
# Flitetec

> Aerospace manufacturer and distributor of aircraft interior components and assemblies for civil and military fixed-wing and rotary platforms.

## What they do

Flitetec Limited was established in 1985 and is headquartered in Amersham, Buckinghamshire, with additional operations in Hampshire and south-west France. The company designs, manufactures, repairs, overhauls, sources, and integrates aircraft interior products for both civil and military aircraft, including fixed-wing and helicopter platforms. Flitetec also operates an aerospace distribution service, and holds CAA Part 21G manufacture approval, Part 145 maintenance approval, and EASA Part 145 maintenance approval, with quality management certified to AS9100 D by Lloyds Register.

## Products and solutions

- **Aircraft interior manufacture and repair** - design, manufacture, and overhaul of aircraft interior components including seating products, galley assemblies, lavatory modules, fairings, meal trays, and specialist interior fittings for civil and military platforms.
- **Aerospace distribution** - sourcing and distribution of aviation components and products for defence and commercial aircraft applications across approximately 100 countries.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.name: Flowcopter Ltd
companies.summary: Scottish developer of hydraulic-drive heavy-lift cargo drones for long-range missions.
companies.body:
# Flowcopter

> Scottish developer of hydraulic-drive heavy-lift cargo drones for long-range missions.

## What they do

Flowcopter designs and manufactures heavy-lift uncrewed air systems built around digital hydraulic drive technology, a spin-out from Artemis Intelligent Power. Its platforms are aimed at markets traditionally served by helicopters, including offshore logistics, renewables, agriculture, emergency response and humanitarian aid. The company pairs combustion engines with digital displacement pumps feeding hydraulic motors that regulate propeller speed.

## Products and solutions

- **FC100** - heavy-lift cargo drone designed to carry payloads up to 100 kg over distances around 200 km with multi-hour endurance, positioned as a lower-fuel alternative to helicopters.

## Recent news

- **2024-05** - Completed flight testing of its heavy-lift cargo drone at HCA Airport in Denmark, its European test base. [source](https://dronelife.com/2024/05/23/flowcopter-successfully-tests-heavylift-cargo-drone-at-hca-airport/)
- **2024-01** - Secured close to £1m from Scottish Enterprise to develop greener heavy-lift industrial drones. [source](https://www.suasnews.com/2024/01/flowcopter-lands-funding-from-scottish-enterprise-to-develop-greener-greater-drones-to-rival-helicopters/)

companies.name: Flyby Technology Limited
companies.summary: York-based developer and manufacturer of armed VTOL UAVs for military and government customers, alongside professional drone training.
companies.body:
# Flyby Technology

> York-based developer and manufacturer of armed VTOL UAVs for military and government customers, alongside professional drone training.

## What they do

Flyby Technology designs and manufactures vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) unmanned air systems for military, government and commercial customers. The company's flagship platform is the JACKAL, an all-electric VTOL UAV capable of carrying and firing air-to-surface missiles. Flyby also operates as a Civil Aviation Authority-authorised Recognised Assessment Entity, providing professional beyond-visual-line-of-sight (BVLOS) drone pilot training delivered by former RAF pilots.

## Products and solutions

- **JACKAL** - all-electric VTOL UAV with approximately 80 nautical miles range and up to 65 kg payload capacity; demonstrated firing Thales Lightweight Multirole Missiles (LMMs) from a hover. Flyby holds full UK manufacturing and export rights to the design.
- **Havoc** - larger planned variant with hybrid propulsion for extended range and heavier weapon carriage.
- **Military drone training** - BVLOS pilot training programme written and delivered by ex-RAF qualified weapons instructors, aimed at military and government operators.
- **Military consultancy and design** - bespoke UAV design and capability development for defence customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-12** - Awarded UK MoD contract to supply JACKAL UAVs for a force-protection demonstration of autonomous battlefield systems (Internet of Battlespace Things), with initial delivery before 31 March 2026. [source](https://www.flightglobal.com/military-uavs/flyby-scores-uk-mod-contract-for-airborne-effectors-as-part-of-force-protection-demonstration/165502.article)
- **2023-09** - Taiwan signed a contract for 160 JACKAL drones, the platform's first export order. [source](https://www.flightglobal.com/defence/jackal-armed-uav-scores-first-order-with-160-unit-taiwan-deal/154979.article)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/flyby-technology

companies.name: FNH UK Limited
companies.summary: The UK's only onshore manufacturer of military rifles, machine guns and weapon mounts, supplying the UK Ministry of Defence and law enforcement agencies.
companies.body:
# FN UK

> The UK's only onshore manufacturer of military rifles, machine guns and weapon mounts, supplying the UK Ministry of Defence and law enforcement agencies.

## What they do

FNH UK Limited (trading as FN UK) is the UK-based subsidiary of FN Herstal, part of the FN Browning Group, owned by the Wallonia regional government of Belgium. Operating from a facility in Erith, Kent, FN UK manufactures small-calibre weapons and weapon mounts under licence for the UK MoD and UK law enforcement customers. The company was formerly Manroy Engineering, acquired by FN Herstal in 2017. FN UK holds an in-house design and engineering team and CNC, paint, chrome finishing, and phosphating capabilities.

## Products and solutions

- **.50 M2/HMG** - heavy machine gun manufactured for UK Armed Forces.
- **GPMG/MAG 58** - general-purpose machine gun manufactured for UK Armed Forces.
- **5.56mm rifles** - military assault rifles manufactured in the UK.
- **Weapon mounts** - vehicle and platform weapon mounts designed and manufactured to UK MoD requirements.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Silver award in the Armed Forces Covenant Employer Recognition Scheme; working towards Gold status.

## Recent news

- **2025** - Celebrating 50 years of UK manufacturing operations, supported by parent FN Herstal's 135-year heritage in small-calibre weapons design.
- **2024** - DE&S awarded FN UK a £20 million contract to upgrade the UK Armed Forces' Heavy Machine Gun, with deliveries staged between March 2026 and March 2028. [source](https://des.mod.uk/des-awards-fn-uk-20m-contract-to-upgrade-uk-armed-forces-heavy-machine-gun/)

companies.name: Forcys Limited
companies.summary: Maritime defence company delivering integrated underwater sensing, communications and payload solutions for navies and defence organisations worldwide.
companies.body:
# Forcys

> Maritime defence company delivering integrated underwater sensing, communications and payload solutions for navies and defence organisations worldwide.

## What they do

Forcys integrates, delivers and supports customer-shaped solutions for underwater and maritime defence operations, drawing on 50 years of combined domain expertise across its constituent businesses. The company brings together technologies covering sonar, optical systems, navigation, communications and command and control into payloads for unmanned underwater vehicles (UUVs), remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) and surface platforms. It serves navies, government defence agencies, and critical national infrastructure protection programmes across the UK, US, Australia and allied nations.

Forcys is part of the Covelya Group. In February 2026, Wavefront Systems formally merged with Chelsea Technologies and Forcys, with all three operating under the Forcys brand.

## Products and solutions

- **Sentinel IDS** - intruder detection sonar capable of detecting, tracking and classifying divers and UUVs at ranges up to 1,000m and 1,500m respectively; described as the world's most deployed underwater intruder detection sonar.
- **Solstice MAS** - multi-aperture sonar for high-resolution seabed mapping on AUV platforms.
- **Vigilant FLS** - forward-looking sonar for obstacle avoidance and navigation.
- **Sentry IMS** - intruder monitoring system for protected area surveillance.
- **Equinox** - remotely operated towed vehicle for mine countermeasures operations.
- **ROV and UUV payloads** - integrated sensor suites for underwater identification, ISR and environmental assessment missions.
- **Secure diver communications** - encrypted acoustic communications for diver and subsurface operations.
- **Tactical modules** - containerised expeditionary solutions for rapid deployment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Armed Forces Covenant Bronze Award (Covelya Group)

## Recent news

- **2026-06-17** - Forcys awarded contract to deploy Sentinel IDS across multiple critical national infrastructure sites for an allied nation. [Offshore Source](https://offshoresource.com/news/company-updates/forcys-wins-key-contract-to-protect-multiple-critical-national-infrastructure-sites)
- **2026-02** - Wavefront Systems formally merged into Forcys alongside Chelsea Technologies, consolidating underwater sensing capabilities under a single brand. [Ocean Science & Technology](https://www.oceansciencetechnology.com/news/wavefront-systems-joins-forcys-group-merger-strengthening-underwater-solutions/)
- **2026-02** - Forcys and QinetiQ signed a partnership to develop deployable underwater test ranges and systems in support of Australian maritime assurance. [Naval Technology](https://www.naval-technology.com/news/qinetiq-forcys-australia-maritime/)
- **2026-03** - Kraken Robotics announced it is acquiring Covelya Group, the parent company of Forcys. [Offshore Source](https://offshoresource.com/news/finance/kraken-robotics-to-acquire-covelya-group-limited/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/forcys-ltd

companies.name: Ford Aerospace Limited
companies.summary: Specialist manufacturer of precision components, laminated shims and assemblies for aerospace, defence and high-technology industries.
companies.body:
# Ford Aerospace Limited

> Specialist manufacturer of precision components, laminated shims and assemblies for aerospace, defence and high-technology industries.

## What they do

Ford Aerospace manufactures and supplies precision machined and fabricated components, assemblies, and adjustable laminated shim products to the aerospace and defence supply chain. The company holds approved supplier status with several major defence and aerospace primes including BAE Systems, Leonardo, Airbus, GKN, Moog Aerospace, Rolls-Royce and Safran. Its portfolio covers surface-bonded and edge-bonded parts in metallic and non-metallic materials, with customisable shim products that replace traditional shim sets in complex assembly applications.

Founded in 1910 and incorporated in 1944, the company is part of the SPIROL Group, a global manufacturer of engineered fasteners, disc springs and shims.

## Products and solutions

- **Precision machined components** - make-to-print components for aerospace and defence assemblies.
- **Laminated shim products** - adjustable and peelable surface and edge-bonded shims in metallic and non-metallic materials, in customisable thicknesses and lamination configurations.
- **Fabricated assemblies** - fabricated metal parts and sub-assemblies for high-technology industry customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, AS/EN 9100 (Aviation, Space and Defence Quality Management System)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Make UK Defence, North West Aerospace Alliance, North East Aerospace Alliance

companies.body:
# Forepoint Limited

> Creative consultancy specialising in brand, digital and motion communications for defence, nuclear, energy and other critical industries.

## What they do

Forepoint is a creative consultancy with over 30 years of experience working with critical industries including defence, nuclear, energy, and safety sectors. The company delivers strategic communications programmes covering brand strategy and development, digital and web, and motion and video production. It works with clients at times of significant change, including major transformation programmes, cultural change initiatives, supply chain communications, and public and stakeholder engagement. Forepoint holds Cyber Essentials and Cyber Essentials Plus certification.

Defence clients include BAE Systems, with whom Forepoint has worked since 2006 as one of eight agencies on BAE Systems' design roster, supporting air, land and sea divisions. The company also works with the Dreadnought Alliance, AWE, and the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority.

## Products and solutions

- **Brand** - brand strategy, brand development, communications, design services and exhibitions for defence and critical industry clients.
- **Digital** - digital strategy, website development, hosting and maintenance, app development and social media.
- **Motion** - video production, 2D and 3D animation, scripting and immersive content for mission-critical communications.

companies.name: Form1 Partners Ltd
companies.summary: Technology commercialisation partner connecting defence and security scale-ups with established corporates and primes.
companies.body:
# Form1 Partners Ltd

> Technology commercialisation partner connecting defence and security scale-ups with established corporates and primes.

## What they do

Form1 Partners is a commercialisation consultancy focused on B2B technology scale-ups and the corporates and primes that seek to work with them. The company has a specific defence and national security practice, staffed by personnel with backgrounds spanning MOD, RAF, Strategic Command, jHub and industry primes. It works with scale-ups to build partnerships with established defence corporates, and with corporates to access and qualify emerging technology from the SME and start-up market.

Form1 was appointed by Leonardo UK as its SME Collaboration Partner to run the Leonardo SME Collaboration Partner Programme, launched in March 2025. The programme identifies and operationalises long-term partnerships between Leonardo's UK business and defence and security SMEs, with the aim of delivering sovereign capability to UK defence and allied forces.

## Products and solutions

- **Corporate partnership advisory** - identifying, structuring and closing technology partnerships between scale-ups and established defence and security corporates.
- **SME programme management** - managing structured SME collaboration programmes on behalf of primes, including sourcing, qualification and relationship management.
- **VC and investor advisory** - supporting venture capital investors with defence sector access and qualification of portfolio company commercial opportunities.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-03** - Leonardo UK launched its SME Collaboration Partner Programme with Form1 Partners as the appointed commercialisation partner; the first partnership under the programme paired Leonardo with Faculty AI. [Army Technology](https://www.army-technology.com/news/leonardos-sme-programme-uk/)

# Fortinet, Inc.

> Cyber security vendor providing network and platform security, with a UK operating company serving public sector and defence.

## What they do

Fortinet is a cyber security company founded in 2000 and headquartered in Sunnyvale, California. It develops firewalls, secure networking, SASE and security operations products, integrated through its Security Fabric platform. The UK operating company, Fortinet UK Limited (Companies House 04570027), serves customers across UK industry and government, including public sector and defence applications.

## Products and solutions

- **FortiGate / FortiOS** - next-generation firewall hardware and operating system. FortiOS 8.0 was announced in 2026 with capabilities for AI security and SASE.
- **Security Fabric** - integrated platform connecting Fortinet's security and networking products.
- **Defence solutions** - products positioned to extend cyber security across the battlespace at the operational level.

companies.name: Foster & Freeman Limited
companies.summary: UK designer and manufacturer of forensic examination equipment for questioned documents, fingerprints, trace evidence, identity verification, and border control.
companies.body:
# Foster + Freeman

> UK designer and manufacturer of forensic examination equipment for questioned documents, fingerprints, trace evidence, identity verification, and border control.

## What they do

Foster + Freeman designs and manufactures systems for the forensic examination of questioned documents, the detection and enhancement of latent fingerprints, and the elemental analysis of trace evidence. The company also produces identity verification and border control technology for travel document authentication. Products are built to order at the company's headquarters in Evesham, Worcestershire, and exported to more than 170 countries. End-users include the Ministry of Defence, law enforcement agencies, border forces, and military organisations worldwide.

## Products and solutions

- **VSC Range (Video Spectral Comparator)** - Workstations for forensic examination of identity, travel, and questioned documents; used at borders and in forensic laboratories.
- **Crime-lite Range** - Forensic alternate light sources for crime scene examination and fingerprint evidence recovery, including handheld and laboratory imaging systems.
- **DCS 6** - Fingermark imaging workstation providing multi-spectral photography for latent print capture and enhancement.
- **VSC ForeFront** - Portable triple-layer identity authentication device for frontline border operations.
- **ffTA (Trace Analysis System)** - Laboratory platform for glass refractive index measurement, Raman spectroscopy, and micro spectrometry of trace evidence.
- **CBRNE and TSCM solutions** - Tactical products for chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosive environments, and technical surveillance countermeasures.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-04-01** - Launched VSC ForeFront, a triple-layer identity authentication device for frontline border operations. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/fosterfreeman-launches-vsc-forefront-triple-layer-identity-authentication-for-frontline-operations/)
- **2026-03-01** - Announced ContraSCAN detection technology targeting the prison drug detection market. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/fosterfreeman-to-tackle-prison-drug-crisis-with-launch-of-contrascan-detection-technology/)
- **2026-03-01** - Supported the JSaRC Immersive Experience at Security and Policing 2026. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/security-policing-2026-supporting-the-jsarc-lmmersive-experience/)

## Links

companies.name: FR Aviation Ltd t/a Draken Europe
companies.summary: Provider of adversary air, electronic warfare and operational readiness training services to the UK MoD and NATO allies, operating as FR Aviation Ltd.
companies.body:
# Draken Europe

> Provider of adversary air, electronic warfare and operational readiness training services to the UK MoD and NATO allies, operating as FR Aviation Ltd.

## What they do

Draken Europe is the trading name of FR Aviation Ltd, a UK entity with roots in 1985 when it was established to deliver air defence training for the Royal Navy following the Falklands conflict. FR Aviation became part of Cobham Plc and was acquired by Draken International in 2020, uniting Draken's US-based adversary air expertise with FR Aviation's live electronic warfare training capability. The company operates from Bournemouth Airport (Hurn) and Teesside Airport, delivering mission-critical training services to government and military customers in the UK and more than 25 nations. Draken International is majority-owned by private equity specialist Blackstone.

## Products and solutions

- **Red Force** - an integrated multi-domain adversary training service combining Adversary Air, Electronic Warfare, Surface Threats, Uncrewed Systems and Situational Awareness elements.
- **Sword (Adversary Air)** - contracted aggressor air services using ex-military aircraft including the L-159E Honey Badger and Dassault Falcon 20, replicating enemy tactics for Gen 4 and Gen 5 operators.
- **Shield (Electronic Warfare)** - live electronic attack, threat simulation, communications jamming and silent target capabilities.
- **Sting (Surface Threats)** - surface threat replication including multiple emitters and platforms with representative signatures.
- **Shadow (Uncrewed Systems)** - uncrewed threat replication services addressing the growing demand for drone threat training.
- **Phantom Sky (Situational Awareness)** - digital backbone of the Red Force suite, providing situational awareness data across the training environment.
- **Aircraft MRO and engineering** - maintenance, repair and overhaul services for the company's own fleet and external customers.
- **Search and rescue and surveillance** - specialist aviation services for government customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025-01-29** - Awarded £173 million Interim Medium Speed Operational Readiness Training Services (IMSORTS) contract by the UK MoD, running from January 2025, delivering training for the RAF and Royal Navy from Teesside Airport and Bournemouth. [source](https://aviationweek.com/defense/light-attack-advanced-training/draken-europe-provide-training-uk-royal-air-force-navy)
- **2026-04** - Expanded Red Force adversary training provision in response to growing demand for electronic warfare expertise. [source](https://www.flightglobal.com/defence/2026/04/draken-steps-up-red-force-adversary-training-provision-as-demand-for-electronic-warfare-expertise-soars/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.draken.aero
