companies.summary: Manufacturer of thermal batteries and power components for missile, munitions and space applications.
companies.body:
# Missiles & Space Batteries Ltd

> Manufacturer of thermal batteries and power components for missile, munitions and space applications.

## What they do

Missiles & Space Batteries Ltd (MSB) designs and manufactures thermal batteries for defence and space programmes. The business traces back to the MSA Catalyst Division established in Scotland in the 1950s and became part of ASB Group in 1996, taking the Missiles & Space Batteries name. It operates from Coatbridge in Lanarkshire as one of the ASB Group's battery businesses, alongside ASB and ATB. Thermal batteries are primary lithium batteries that remain inert until activated and are maintenance-free over long storage periods, a property suited to munitions and weapons that may sit in storage for years before use. The site holds design, manufacture and logistics capability on one location, including a dry room area for powder processing, pellet manufacture and battery build.

## Products and solutions

- **Thermal batteries** - power sources for tactical missiles and guided rockets, supplying seekers, control electronics, safety and arming units, actuators, telemetry and self-destruction systems.
- **Munitions and platform power** - battery power for bomb kits, artillery, countermeasures, underwater defence torpedoes (UDT), ejection seats and emergency power systems.
- **Space launcher batteries** - thermal batteries qualified for space launch applications, including use on the Ariane 6 and Vega-C launchers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, EN 9100 Rev D, Cyber Essentials Plus.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Links

- Website: https://www.asb-group.com

# Mistral AI
> Paris-based foundation-model company building open and proprietary LLMs, with a growing defence vertical targeting European sovereign AI workloads.

## What they do
Mistral AI is Europe's leading AI lab, developing open and proprietary large language models and positioning itself for sovereign and defence AI workloads across European NATO nations. The company has formed a strategic partnership with Helsing focused on vision-language-action models for next-generation defence systems. A framework agreement with the French Ministry of Defence was announced in January 2026.

## Products and solutions
- **Mistral LLMs** - Open and proprietary large language models, including frontier models intended for sovereign and classified deployment.
- **Vision-language-action models** - Joint development with Helsing combining language understanding, visual perception and decision-making outputs for defence platforms.

companies.body:
# MKC Training Services Ltd

> Defence-focused training provider delivering construction, engineering and project management programmes from foundation to degree level, primarily for the MOD.

## What they do

MKC Training Services designs, manages, delivers and assesses artisan, technical and professional training and qualifications, from foundation level to Master's degree, primarily for the British Army's Royal Engineers. It delivers training at the Royal School of Military Engineering, Brompton Barracks, Chatham, as part of the Holdfast Training Services consortium under a Ministry of Defence public-private partnership. It is a wholly owned subsidiary of MidKent College, with profits reinvested into the college and local community. The company states it designs bespoke training solutions end to end, and also delivers face-to-face, virtual and classroom-based instruction directly, including training the trainers and assessing trainees.

## Products and solutions

- **APM Project Fundamentals Qualification and Project Management Qualification** - introductory and full project management qualifications based on the APM Body of Knowledge.
- **PRINCE2 and PRINCE2 Agile (Foundation and Practitioner)** - project management qualifications.
- **Managing Successful Programmes (MSP)** - programme management qualification, Foundation and Practitioner.
- **AgilePM and APMG Change Management** - agile project management and organisational change qualifications.
- **Management of Risk (M_o_R)** - risk management qualification.
- **Construction and engineering courses** - including scaffolding inspection, AutoCAD and additive manufacturing introductions, delivered at foundation to degree level.
- **Bespoke training design** - e-learning, blended and immersive (AR/VR/MR) training solutions, plus train-the-trainer services.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001; City & Guilds Approved Centre; PeopleCert Accredited Training Organisation (PRINCE2, PRINCE2 Agile, M_o_R, MSP).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025** - MKC Training reported milestone results at the halfway point of its Royal School of Military Engineering contract, including a 100% attributable pass rate and 100% trainee satisfaction. [source](https://www.mkctraining.com/who-we-are-our-history)
- **2025** - MKC Training received its 14th consecutive RoSPA Gold Award, plus a RoSPA President's Award. [source](https://www.mkctraining.com/who-we-are-our-history)

## Links

# Mobifon Communications Ltd

> Secure and discreet mobile communications solutions for personnel operating in hostile environments.

## What they do

Mobifon provides secure mobile communications and J6 advisory services for defence and government clients, drawing on over 25 years of experience in the telecommunications sector. The company was founded by ex-service personnel who identified critical shortcomings in mobile communications and J6 policy during deployments to hostile environments. Beyond defence, Mobifon operates as a business telecommunications provider based in Tamworth, Warwickshire, delivering hosted telephony, mobile networks, mobile device management and managed connectivity services to enterprise, education, health and government customers.

## Products and solutions

- **Secure mobile handsets and Cypher SIMs** - encrypted mobile devices and SIM products for military units, defence contractors and organisations operating in hostile or high-risk environments, including deployments in Ukraine.
- **J6 advisory services** - communications policy and planning advice for defence and government clients, drawing on the founders' operational J6 experience.
- **Hosted telephony and managed connectivity** - cloud-hosted telephony, mobile device management, multi-network mobile services and business connectivity for the wider enterprise market.

# Modux Limited

> Cyber security, software development and simulation consultancy serving UK defence, national security and government programmes.

## What they do

Founded in 2008, Modux is a technical consultancy delivering cyber security, software development, and 3D simulation and serious games across defence, government and private industry. The company employs technical specialists with backgrounds in large-scale government and defence programmes, and works across defence and aerospace, finance and telecommunications. Modux is an NCSC-approved CHECK company, delivering penetration testing and security assessment services under the government-accredited CHECK scheme, alongside broader technical consulting and assurance work.

## Products and solutions

- **Penetration testing and assurance** - CHECK-accredited penetration testing and security assessment of IT systems, covering application, infrastructure and mobile security, delivered by consultants holding CREST and Offensive Security certifications.
- **Red team simulation** - realistic enterprise attack simulations, using both publicly available and in-house tools to model the methods of external attackers and malicious insiders.
- **Software development** - bespoke software for systems used across defence, government and private-sector programmes, delivered directly and through industry partners.
- **3D simulation and serious games** - interactive simulation and gaming environments supporting training, data analysis and simulation for enterprise, critical national infrastructure and defence clients.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

# Mollis Group

> Multi-cloud security consultancy helping organisations operate securely in cloud environments.

## What they do

Mollis Group is a multi-cloud security consultancy that helps organisations operate securely in the cloud, providing security guidance it describes as simple, consistent and transparent. The company organises its work around three areas: building security into development (DevSecOps), assuring systems through testing and framework compliance, and operating secure multi-cloud environments on an ongoing basis.

## Products and solutions

- **Penetration testing** - ethical hacking services, from one-off tests to embedded specialists working continuously within agile teams.
- **Threat hunting** - continuous monitoring for threats across client environments.
- **Incident response** - forensics, logging and monitoring support following a security incident.
- **Vulnerability assessment (Swordfish)** - vulnerability assessment as a service.
- **Security architecture services** - design work covering identity and access management, data encryption and network segmentation.
- **TSCM sweeps (Cyber Sweep)** - technical surveillance countermeasures using government-accredited equipment, alongside wireless and RF assurance.
- **OSINT and dark web investigations** - open-source and dark web intelligence gathering.
- **Governance, risk and compliance advisory** - cloud governance, risk management and compliance support, including cloud financial governance and fractional security leadership (vCISO/vCTO-style advisory).

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud, JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://mollis-group.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/mollisgroup

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Sources: company website (mollis-group.co.uk, Security Services, TSCM Sweeps, Security Architecture Services pages), UK Government Digital Marketplace G-Cloud 14 listing, Companies House (company 11853522), LinkedIn. Note: D3IP/Defence BattleLab/Dorset Innovation Park membership could not be corroborated from any public source and has been left out of the entry pending direct confirmation.

# MonsterCAM Composite Systems Ltd

> UK composite tooling and component manufacturer based at the Daedalus enterprise zone, Lee-on-the-Solent, serving the defence, marine and industrial sectors.

## What they do

MonsterCAM Composite Systems Ltd operates a large-scale five-axis CNC machining facility at Daedalus Drive, Lee-on-the-Solent, Hampshire, offering end-to-end composite tooling and component solutions. The company describes itself as specialising in the defence, marine and industrial sectors, building composite components ranging from uncrewed aerial vehicles to submarines. It sits within a wider maritime, aviation and defence SME community on the former HMS Daedalus site, with direct slipway access for transporting oversized work by barge.

## Products and solutions

- **CNC machining** - five-axis CNC machining of patterns, moulds and tooling.
- **Composite mouldings** - carbon fibre and epoxy/resin infusion builds, including pre-preg processes.
- **Metrology** - dimensional inspection and quality verification of composite components.
- **Painting and finishing** - surface finishing for composite parts.
- **Tooling** - design and build of composite tooling for defence and marine programmes.
- Named defence-sector work described by the company includes composite hulls and structures for uncrewed underwater and surface vessels, survey submarines, and minesweeper tooling and build.

companies.summary: Manufacturer of flight control systems and precision motion control products for military and commercial aircraft.
companies.body:
# Moog Aircraft Group

> Manufacturer of flight control systems and precision motion control products for military and commercial aircraft.

## What they do

Moog Aircraft Group is the aerospace and defence operating group of Moog Inc, a US-headquartered precision motion control company. The group describes itself as a designer, manufacturer and integrator of flight control systems for military and commercial aircraft, with products used across the majority of the commercial aircraft market. Its UK site at Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, houses the Aircraft Controls Group alongside a separate Industrial Systems Group, and covers design, development, test, certification and in-service support for flight control equipment.

## Products and solutions

- **Primary and secondary flight controls** - actuation systems covering the full "stick to tail" control chain for civil and military aircraft.
- **High lift controls and control electronics** - systems supporting aircraft high-lift surfaces and their associated electronics.
- **Servo valves and manifolds** - electrically operated servovalves and manifolds controlling hydraulic fluid flow for precise actuator position, velocity, pressure and force control; military servo valves are designed and manufactured in-country, with civil servo valves qualified at Tewkesbury for final-phase manufacture and assembly.
- **Fuel metering interface devices** - components for aircraft engine fuel systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026** - Moog expanded its Gloucestershire engineering team at its Tewkesbury facility, recruiting for specialist roles including quality engineer and senior manufacturing engineer to support aerospace programmes and manufacturing growth. [source](https://www.soglos.com/news/business/aerospace-giant-moog-expands-gloucestershire-engineering-team-at-50-million-smart-factory/27093/)
- **2025/2026** - Moog opened its 2026 apprenticeship intake at Tewkesbury, covering Level 3 and Level 4/5 manufacturing and engineering apprenticeships delivered via the Gloucestershire Engineering Training centre. [source](https://www.soglos.com/news/business/heres-how-budding-aerospace-apprentices-can-earn-while-they-learn-at-a-50-million-smart-factory/26163/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.moog.com/markets/aircraft.html

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Sources: moog.com (Aircraft market page, Aircraft Group news), ADS Group member directory, Companies House (Moog Controls Limited, company 01171948), Tewkesbury Business Council case study, trade press (soglos.com). The UK Tewkesbury facility is a 209,000 sq ft site opened in 2023; Moog also operates separate UK sites at Luton (Moog Fernau) and Reading (Moog Reading), each carried as a separate Tally entry.

# Moog Fernau Ltd

> UK supplier of ground-based navigation aids and defence communications and datalink systems, part of Moog Inc.

## What they do

Moog Fernau, based in Luton, is a supplier of ground-based navigation aids with experience across all aspects of navigation. Its products and systems have been installed for civil aviation, maritime authorities and military forces worldwide, with stated customers including the US Navy, UK MOD, US Air Force and US Marine Corps. Trading as part of Moog Inc, the business descends from Fernau Avionics, which became part of Moog in 2009.

## Products and solutions

- **DME** - distance measuring equipment, a ground-based navigation aid.
- **VHF/UHF DF and Naval DF** - direction-finding systems for aviation and maritime use.
- **Auto-Triangulation (Auto-T)** - automatic triangulation for locating transmissions.
- **TACAN** - fixed tactical air navigation, with man-portable, mobile and deployable variants.
- **Mode-S radar transmitters** - secondary surveillance radar transmitters.
- **Tarsier** - automatic foreign object debris (FOD) detection system for civil and military airfields.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

# Moog Reading

> UK components and systems manufacturing operation of Moog Inc, supplying slip rings and power/data transfer solutions to defence, aerospace and space platforms.

## What they do

Moog Reading Limited, trading as part of Moog's Power and Data Division, is based at Suttons Business Park, Earley, Reading. The site's registered scope covers the design, manufacture and repair of electrical sliding contacts, slip ring separates and packaged units, and fibre optic rotary joints. Moog describes the wider Power and Data Division as supplying power and data transfer solutions, motion control and electronic products designed to meet the technical and environmental requirements of the defence and space markets, with products used on armoured vehicles, electro-optic and infrared systems, helicopters and space satellites.

## Products and solutions

- **Slip rings** - electro-optic system twist capsules, helicopter slip rings, miniature slip ring capsules, propeller slip rings and vehicular slip rings, used in applications including target acquisition systems and tank turrets.
- **Fibre optic rotary joints** - rotary joint products for data transfer across rotating interfaces.
- **Electrical sliding contacts and packaged units** - components supplied under the site's certified manufacturing scope.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** EN 9100:2018 / ISO 9001:2015 (BSI-certified quality management system for the Reading site).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group (listed as Moog Components Group Ltd).

## Links

Public information verified 2026-07-05. No Reading-specific news from the last 12 months was found in public sources.

# Morshead Consulting Ltd

> Defence analysis, experimentation and programme transformation consultancy working with Dstl, the UK Armed Forces and industry partners.

## What they do

Morshead Consulting is an independent consultancy delivering analysis, experimentation and programme transformation to Dstl, the UK Armed Forces and industry partners, working across Land, C4ISR and Cyber domains. The company states it was founded in 2020 to provide high-quality analysis, experimentation and transformation for the UK MOD, industry and government agencies, positioning itself as a supplier of advice and support rather than technology or product.

## Products and solutions

- **Operational analysis** - concept assessment, business cases and cost-benefit analysis, and Defence Lines of Development (DLOD) analysis for programmes spanning command and control, electronic warfare and one-way-effector systems, for customers including Dstl and Army HQ.
- **Experimentation** - warfare development, modelling and simulation, and wargaming support, including concept of employment and tactics, techniques and procedures development, for customers including Dstl, Army HQ and the Land Warfare Centre.
- **Programme transformation** - programme start-up advice, benefits realisation, enterprise architecture and embedded "customer friend" support for defence transformation programmes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.name: Morson Praxis
companies.summary: Engineering consultancy delivering integrated design, analysis and industrial capability across aerospace and defence, from concept through to certification and in-service support.
companies.body:
# Morson Praxis

> Engineering consultancy delivering integrated design, analysis and industrial capability across aerospace and defence, from concept through to certification and in-service support.

## What they do

Morson Praxis is an engineering consultancy delivering integrated design, analysis and industrial capability across aerospace and defence, marine and manufacturing, civil and energy, and digital sectors. The company supports programmes from concept through to certification, production and in-service support, operating within highly regulated environments including Part 21J and military frameworks. Morson Praxis was formed from the consolidation of legacy Morson Group engineering brands, including Morson Projects, Ematics, Waldeck, Orange Solutions and Astraeus.

## Products and solutions

- **Aerospace and defence engineering** - structural design and stress analysis, tooling development, composite structures and systems integration across the product lifecycle; manufacturing engineering; high-security site engineering including MOD installations.
- **Marine and naval engineering** - naval architecture, structural finite element analysis, hydrodynamic assessment, signature modelling, blast and impact analysis, and ship stability analysis, with named work on the Dreadnought submarine, Type 26 frigate and Type 45 destroyer repowering programmes.
- **Submarines and underwater systems** - naval architecture, structural assessment, materials selection, corrosion analysis, hydrodynamics and signatures, electrical/control and instrumentation (EC&I) integration, shock analysis and control software development.
- **Land systems** - military land systems design, vehicle platform engineering, soldier systems, defence infrastructure, through-life support and signature management.
- **Civil aviation certification** - UK CAA Part 21J Design Organisation Approval, covering major and minor modifications, supplementary type certificates, structural analysis and electrical/avionic design certification.
- **Electrical and avionic design** - specialist RF, EMC and WiFi support, and ground, flight and EMC testing.
- **Systems engineering and integration** - cargo handling, flight controls, fuel systems, HVAC, hydraulic assemblies and landing gear, for clients including Airbus, BAE Systems, Rolls-Royce and Leonardo.
- **Manufacturing engineering** - CNC programming, tooling design and manufacture, and collaborative robot (cobot) integration.
- **Digital and technology consulting** - data strategy and governance, analytics, AI and machine learning (predictive modelling, computer vision, generative AI), cyber security and assurance, software engineering, and digital capture including UAV aerial surveys and a 3D digital twin visualisation environment (EVE).
- **Supportability engineering** - Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) across defence and industrial programmes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** RM6356 Construction Professional Services 2 (CPS2)
- **Accreditations:** ITAR, AS/EN/JISQ 9100, ISO 9001, ISO 27001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 19650-2, ISO 20000, Cyber Essentials Plus, Part 21J, UKAS, PDRA01
- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Nuclear Industry Association, Institution of Engineering and Technology, Royal Institution of Naval Architects, West of England Aerospace Forum

## Recent news

- **2026-06** - Appointed to the RM6356 Construction Professional Services 2 (CPS2) framework, covering professional services across infrastructure, built environment and defence. [source](https://www.newcivilengineer.com/latest/government-commercial-agency-awards-places-on-4-2bn-construction-professional-services-framework-26-06-2026/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.morson-praxis.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/morson-praxis/

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Sources: company website (morson-praxis.com, including Aerospace & Defence, Marine, Submarines & Underwater Systems, Land Systems, Civil Aviation, Systems Engineering & Integration, and Credentials pages), ADS Group member directory, New Civil Engineer, Companies House (registered entity Morson Projects Limited, company 01457298), LinkedIn. Note: the operative company website is morson-praxis.com; the.co.uk address previously recorded did not resolve to the live site. AI, cyber security and data capability tags reflect general digital-consulting service lines described on the company's own site rather than defence-specific service lines; treat accordingly.

companies.body:
# MSI-Defence Systems Ltd

> UK land and naval weapon systems specialist, the only UK-based medium calibre naval gun system manufacturer.

## What they do

MSI-Defence Systems (MSI-DS) is a UK-based land and naval weapon systems specialist and a world leader in small and medium calibre naval gun systems. It is the only UK-based medium calibre system manufacturer. The company supplies lightweight automated weapon mounts to the Royal Navy and to over 40 armed forces worldwide, and traces its engineering heritage back more than a century.

## Products and solutions

- **SEAHAWK** - multi-role naval weapon system available as the DS 25/30mm mounting and the lightweight LW 20mm mounting, in service with numerous navies.
- **DS30M Mark 2** - 30mm automated small calibre gun mounting in service with the Royal Navy.
- **TERRAHAWK** - land-based VSHORAD (very short range air defence) system.
- **Stabilised systems** - stabilised mounts and radar mast systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

## What they do

MSM aerospace fabricators manufactures and repairs aerospace components including bleed air ducts, anti-ice piccolo tubes, ozone converters, nacelle fabrications, and fuel, oil and water tanks. The company holds EASA Part 145 approval and offers specialist capabilities in hydroform pressing, bulge forming in titanium and Inconel, 5-axis laser profiling, and laser and resistance welding.

# Musketeer Solutions Ltd

> MOD-registered independent airworthiness consultancy supporting military large transport and ISTAR aircraft platforms since 2006.

## What they do

Musketeer Solutions Ltd (MSL) delivers independent airworthiness support to the MOD and military aircraft industry primes, specialising in large transport and ISTAR platforms. The company is MOD FATS-STS/D&E registered and ISO 9001 certified, with a track record of airworthiness review, on-aircraft system interconnectivity inspection, structural repair assessment, and zonal survey work delivered on time and on cost.

companies.name: Mutable Tactics
companies.summary: UK defence-tech startup founded in August 2024, developing AI decision-layer software for coordinated autonomy of uncrewed defence systems.
companies.body:
# Mutable Tactics
> UK defence-tech startup founded in August 2024, developing AI decision-layer software for coordinated autonomy of uncrewed defence systems.

## What they do
Mutable Tactics builds software described as a "decision-layer" that sits between a human operator and multiple uncrewed systems, allowing air, maritime and ground platforms to coordinate and make local decisions in communications-denied or GPS-denied environments. The company's stated aim is to move beyond the model of one operator controlling one uncrewed system, enabling a single operator to direct multiple platforms at once.

## Products and solutions
- **Decision-layer software** - AI-enabled coordination software for uncrewed aerial, maritime and ground systems operating with degraded communications or navigation.

## Funding
- Closed a pre-seed funding round of approximately £1.6 million ($2.1 million / €1.8 million) in March 2026, led by Seraphim Space with participation from the UK's National Security Strategic Investment Fund, Koro, Entrepreneurs First and Transpose.

## Links
- Website: https://mutabletactics.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/mutabletactics

## Sources
Public information verified 2026-07-05. Companies House registered office confirmed at London, EC2A 4NE. Funding and product detail reported by Tech.eu, UKTN, EU-Startups, New Electronics and the NSSIF (all March 2026).

companies.name: MyDefence
companies.summary: Counter-drone and counter-UAV systems manufacturer combining RF detection technology with rapid-response neutralisation capabilities for military and critical infrastructure protection.
companies.body:
# MyDefence

> Counter-drone and counter-UAV systems manufacturer combining RF detection technology with rapid-response neutralisation capabilities for military and critical infrastructure protection.

## What they do

MyDefence develops next-generation counter-UAS solutions built around advanced radio frequency detection and electronic neutralisation. The company works directly with frontline forces to incorporate real-time operational insights into product design, producing systems certified to ISO 9001 and intended for military operations as well as the protection of airports, corporate facilities and high-security sites.

## Products and solutions

- **Counter-drone detection and neutralisation systems** - RF-based systems for detecting and defeating drone threats in military and critical infrastructure environments.
- **Wingman** - wearable passive RF drone detector covering 200 MHz to 6 GHz, with stated detection in under 10 seconds and threat direction finding.
- **Pitbull** - wearable RF jammer stated to neutralise drone threats by disrupting control-link and GNSS signals; combines with Wingman as "The Soldier Kit".
- **Watchdog 150/202/250** - fixed and vehicle-mounted RF sensors for drone detection.
- **Dobermann 151** - vehicle-mounted RF jammer.
- **Wolfpack** - portable RF drone detector.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001

Nagra Media UK Ltd, based in Cwmbran, is part of the Kudelski Group and trades publicly as NAGRAVISION. companies.name: Nammo UK Ltd
companies.summary: UK site of Nammo, a Norwegian defence and aerospace manufacturer specialising in rocket propulsion and ammunition.
companies.body:
# Nammo UK Ltd

> UK site of Nammo, a Norwegian defence and aerospace manufacturer specialising in rocket propulsion and ammunition.

## What they do

Based at Westcott Venture Park in Buckinghamshire, Nammo UK develops and manufactures chemical propulsion solutions for space and defence applications. The site has been occupied since 1946 and moved into spacecraft rocket engine manufacture in the mid-1990s; Nammo describes itself as a leading provider of spacecraft propulsion solutions for Earth orbit, Moon and interplanetary missions, offering everything from individual components to bespoke, vertically integrated propulsion solutions.

## Products and solutions

- **LEROS rocket engine family** - apogee engines and thrusters, with over 90 flown missions for the LEROS Apogee engines and more than 100 for LEROS thrusters.
- **LEROS 1C** - dual-mode apogee engine supplied to Lockheed Martin for NASA's Lucy mission to the Trojan asteroids.
- **LEROS 4-ET** - provided descent orbit insertion propulsion for Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost lunar lander.
- **RELIANCE** - next-generation throttleable bi-propellant engine, developed at Westcott and selected as the main engine for ESA's Argonaut lunar lander.
- **LMP-103s monopropellant propulsion system** - supplied to Astroscale for the ADRAS-J space debris removal mission.
- **Test facilities** - operates the miniHATF, F-Site and J-Site test facilities, and the UK Space Agency's National Space Propulsion Test Facility.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-03-02** - LEROS 4-ET engine performed the descent burn for Firefly's Blue Ghost lunar lander, the first commercial lunar landing. [source](https://www.nammo.com/location/westcott/)
- **2025** - Selected as main engine supplier for ESA's Argonaut lunar lander with the RELIANCE engine. [source](https://westcottvp.com/news-and-insights/news/westcott-based-nammo-uk-wins-main-engine-supplier-for-esa-argonaut-luna-lander)

## Links

- Website: https://www.nammo.com/location/westcott/
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/nammo-westcott

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Company website (nammo.com), Westcott Venture Park news pages, ADS members directory snapshot (2026-05-15).

companies.name: Nasmyth Bulwell
companies.summary: Precision aerospace manufacturer producing machined parts, kits, airframe assemblies and sub-assemblies from its facility in Pinxton, Nottinghamshire.
companies.body:
# Nasmyth Bulwell

> Precision aerospace manufacturer producing machined parts, kits, airframe assemblies and sub-assemblies from its facility in Pinxton, Nottinghamshire.

## What they do

Nasmyth Bulwell specialises in precision machining of steel, aluminium, nimonics and super alloys using up to 5-axis CAD/CAM technology, producing quality-assured components and assemblies for aerospace and defence customers. Capabilities include fine tolerance grinding, water jet cutting, heat treatment, anodising, non-destructive testing, and paint and plasma finishing. The company operates as part of the Nasmyth Group, drawing on the group's supply chain management to deliver airframe assemblies and kitted parts directly to customers.

## Products and solutions

- **Precision parts, kits and airframe assemblies** - machined and assembled components and sub-assemblies for aerospace and defence platforms.
- **Wet-assembly aerostructure components** - captive nut installation, heat-and-shrink bush installation, and proof-load testing.
- **Welded assemblies** - coded and approved welding for fuel pipes, ground support equipment, satellite drill fixtures, and military vehicle and lightweight rotary aircraft products.
- **Electro-mechanical assemblies** - flight simulator and flat panel trainer seat systems, and repair and overhaul of flight column and throttle box control assemblies.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS9100 Rev D, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, NADCAP Heat Treatment, NADCAP Non-Destructive Testing, UKAS BS/EN ISO 17025 (calibration systems).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-10-28** - Nasmyth Group, the parent company, was sold to Sigma Advanced Systems UK Ltd, a subsidiary of India's Megasoft Limited, with control effective from 1 November 2025. [source](https://www.insidermedia.com/news/midlands/sale-of-500-strong-aerospace-and-defence-engineering-firm-to-indian-group-completes)

## Links

- Website: http://www.bulwell.com
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/nasmyth-bulwell

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Company website (nasmyth.com/companies/nasmyth-bulwell), ADS members directory snapshot (2026-05-15), and reputable business press (Insider Media) on the Nasmyth Group ownership change.

# Nasmyth Coventry

> Advanced assembly manufacturer specialising in low-volume, complex precision structures for aerospace, marine and engineering sectors, part of the Nasmyth Group.

## What they do

Nasmyth Coventry employs around 100 engineers, sheet metal technicians and welding specialists producing large-scale aluminium, stainless steel, titanium and rare alloy assemblies. The company handles structural and turned components to the largest sizes its customers require, and is fully conversant with reverse engineering techniques. It is a long-established manufacturer of complex sheet metal, welded and machined assemblies serving the aero engine, airframe, space and industrial gas turbine sectors, and also provides precision engineering and metal treatment for maritime customers, including assembly and test of complex maritime systems and precision machined components up to two metres in diameter.

## Products and solutions

- **Sheet metal and welded assemblies** - large-scale aluminium, stainless steel, titanium and rare alloy structures for aero engine, airframe and industrial gas turbine customers.
- **Machined and turned components** - precision machining to customer-specified sizes, including reverse engineering of legacy parts.
- **Maritime system assembly and test** - electro-mechanical assembly, system integration and test of maritime systems and fabrications.

companies.summary: Group of precision engineering businesses delivering design, manufacturing, assembly and product support services across mechanical and electro-mechanical engineering for aerospace, defence and marine.
companies.body:
# Nasmyth Group Ltd

> Group of precision engineering businesses delivering design, manufacturing, assembly and product support services across mechanical and electro-mechanical engineering for aerospace, defence and marine.

## What they do

Nasmyth Group describes itself as a full-capability supplier of precision engineering addressing requirements for global aerospace, defence, marine, medical, instrumentation and specialised automotive markets. It brings together a group of customer-orientated precision engineering and metal treatment businesses to offer a single-source solution across design, manufacture, assembly, testing and product support. The group is headquartered in Coventry and employs over 500 people across sites in the UK, Europe and Asia Pacific.

## Products and solutions

- **Complex machining** - precision machining of high-performance metals, including multi-axis milling, turning and mill turning, and CNC grinding, in titanium, aluminium, nimonics and superalloys.
- **Fabrication and welding** - complex sheet metal, welded and machined assemblies and structures, including hydraulic press forming, fusion and resistance welding, and precision fabricated structures up to five tonnes.
- **System integration** - electro-mechanical systems, cable and harness assembly, PCB assembly and electronic test, including assemblies for flight simulator seat installations and control unit repair and overhaul.
- **Metal treatments** - surface finishing and chemical and thermal treatments, including anodising, alkaline zinc, chromic passivation, shot peening and thermal barrier coating.
- **Design, reverse engineering and MRO** - CAD/CAM-supported design and reverse engineering, non-destructive testing, and maintenance, repair and overhaul including AOG support and FAR/EASA Part 145 compliant repairs.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Midlands Aerospace Alliance
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, AS9100 Rev C, ISO 14001, EASA/FAR Part 145, CAA Parts 145 and 21, NADCAP (chemical processing, conventional testing, heat treatment, NDT)

## Recent news

- **2026-06-15** - Nasmyth Group opens a new manufacturing facility in Sri City, India, and confirms delivery on a long-term contract with Rolls-Royce alongside parent company Sigma Advanced Systems. [source](https://www.nasmyth.com/news/nasmyth-group-forges-capability-in-sri-city-india-to-deliver-on-its-300m-long-term-contract-with-rolls-royce-)
- **2025-11-03** - Nasmyth Group is acquired by Sigma Advanced Systems UK Ltd, a defence and security systems manufacturer. [source](https://www.nasmyth.com/news/nasmyth-group-acquired-by-sigma-advanced-systems-uk-ltd-acquisition-strengthens-future-growth-and-innovation)

## Links

companies.summary: Precision ring component manufacturer serving aero engine, marine engine and satellite programmes, part of the Nasmyth Group.
companies.body:
# Nasmyth Henton

> Precision ring component manufacturer serving aero engine, marine engine and satellite programmes, part of the Nasmyth Group.

## What they do

Nasmyth Henton describes itself as a leading supplier to the aero engine, marine and satellite industries, manufacturing ring-machined components. Based in Hinckley, Leicestershire, and part of the Nasmyth Group since 2008, it specialises in the machining and fine grinding of large, complex, sectioned ring and prismatic components, typically 200mm to 1.8-2m in diameter, working with customers from design and material sourcing through to manufacture, assembly and test.

## Products and solutions

- **Ring machined components** - compressor seals, compressor casings, satellite mounting rings, adapter flanges and bearing housings, produced on a five-axis CNC machining facility.
- **Multi-axis milling and turning** - three, four and five-axis CNC milling and turning, including multi-spindle lathes, in titanium, nimonic, stellite, aluminium and other high-temperature alloys.
- **CNC grinding and non-destructive testing** - precision grinding of machined components, plasma coatings, lapping and honing, plus in-house magnetic particle and penetrant inspection.
- **Marine and submarine systems assembly and test** - a specialist assembly and test facility for systems used in marine and submarine environments.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Midlands Aerospace Alliance
- **Accreditations:** AS9100D, ISO 14001, NADCAP NDT

## What they do

Nasmyth IEC specialises in assembly, systems engineering and CNC machining, manufacturing complex parts and assemblies for the aerospace, defence, marine, instrumentation, energy, medical and mobility markets, working in nickel alloys, Inconel, titanium, magnesium and high-performance plastics. The company describes itself as serving space, air, land and sea applications for the defence sector, and holds a specialist facility for assembly and test of systems used in marine and submarine environments.

- **Electro-mechanical and cable and harness assembly** - low-volume, high-mix electronics manufacturing, cable and harness assembly, and PCB assembly for aerospace, defence and oil and gas customers.
- **Assemblies and testing** - aerostructure, welded, electrical and mechanical assemblies including fuel pipes, ground support equipment, satellite drill fixtures, military vehicle components and flight simulator seat installations.

companies.name: National Composites Centre
companies.summary: UK national R&D facility providing industry access to composite engineering capability for aerospace, defence and advanced manufacturing sectors.
companies.body:
# National Composites Centre

> UK national R&D facility providing industry access to composite engineering capability for aerospace, defence and advanced manufacturing sectors.

## What they do

The National Composites Centre (NCC) describes itself as a world-leading innovation organisation that turns research and technology into industrial impact, bridging academia and industry across the product life cycle. It is a not-for-profit centre and one of seven centres in the UK's High Value Manufacturing Catapult, working with organisations from micro-businesses to major primes across aerospace, defence, energy and other sectors, and states it has invested more than £200 million in advanced composites capability.

## Products and solutions

- **Innovation services** - guiding organisations from emerging ideas to real-world application, managing risk and accelerating technology adoption.
- **Technical services** - open-access facilities, equipment and engineering expertise for composite manufacturing development.
- **Supply chain services** - targeted support for SMEs to embed advanced technologies and build business capability.
- **Skills services** - tailored training and workforce development programmes.
- **Manufacturing capability** - braiding, automated fibre placement, high-volume manufacturing, ultra-high-rate deposition, ceramic matrix composites, resin impregnation, and verification and testing facilities.
- **Defence-facing programmes** - the Composites Technology Forum for UK Defence and the Applied Research and Technology Forum for UK Defence, run with defence primes, academia and SMEs, alongside stated work on advanced materials, digital engineering and digital twins.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group, High Value Manufacturing Catapult
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, Airbus AIP103-02-019 approval

## Recent news

- **2026-06-10** - National Composites Centre appoints two new Non-Executive Advisors to its Board. [source](https://www.nccuk.com/insight-impact/two-new-non-executive-advisors-appointed-to-ncc-board/)

companies.name: National Nuclear Laboratory
companies.summary: The UK's national laboratory for nuclear science and technology, serving defence, civil nuclear, and homeland security customers. Now publicly styled the United Kingdom National Nuclear Laboratory (UKNNL) following a December 2024 rebrand.
companies.body:
# National Nuclear Laboratory

> The UK's national laboratory for nuclear science and technology, serving defence, civil nuclear, and homeland security customers. Now publicly styled the United Kingdom National Nuclear Laboratory (UKNNL) following a December 2024 rebrand.

## What they do

National Nuclear Laboratory (NNL), now publicly styled United Kingdom National Nuclear Laboratory (UKNNL), describes its purpose as nuclear science to benefit society. It is the UK's government-owned, operationally independent national laboratory for nuclear fission, covering the complete nuclear fuel cycle from fuel manufacture and power generation through to reprocessing, waste treatment and disposal, and including defence, new nuclear build and homeland security. Its work is organised into four stated focus areas: clean energy, environmental restoration, health and nuclear medicine, and security and non-proliferation.

## Products and solutions

- **Fuel cycle and reactor support** - fuel manufacture, reactor operations support, reprocessing, waste treatment and disposal.
- **Decommissioning and environmental restoration** - support for nuclear site decommissioning programmes, including work at Sellafield.
- **Defence nuclear support** - stated support to the Ministry of Defence on the reactors powering the Royal Navy's submarine fleet, delivered in coordination with Rolls-Royce.
- **Security and non-proliferation** - nuclear site security assessment and design, safeguards support, and delivery of the UK's national support programme to the IAEA.
- **RadBall radiation mapping** - a passive, unpowered 3D radiation-mapping device that collimates radiation onto a radiation-sensitive polymer sphere, later imaged by optical-CT scanning to produce a 3D map of radiation hotspots, used in areas too radioactive or inaccessible for conventional instruments.
- **Central Laboratory and Windscale Laboratory (Sellafield)** - post-irradiation examination and related analytical services supporting plant life extension.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group, AIRTO (Association of Innovation, Research and Technology Organisations), Security Institute
- **Programmes:** IAEA Collaborating Centre for the Advanced Fuel Cycle (designated 2020); UK SMR consortium

## Recent news

- **2026-07** - UKNNL and the University of Manchester sign a Memorandum of Understanding on nuclear science, workforce and technology partnership. [source](https://uknnl.com)
- **2026-02** - UKNNL renews its partnership with Bangor University on nuclear research and North Wales nuclear skills and technology. [source](https://uknnl.com)

companies.name: NavyX
companies.summary: The Royal Navy's Autonomy, Lethality and Innovation accelerator, developing and testing autonomous and novel technology for naval operations.
companies.body:
# NavyX

> The Royal Navy's Autonomy, Lethality and Innovation accelerator, developing and testing autonomous and novel technology for naval operations.

## What they do

NavyX is the Royal Navy's Autonomy, Lethality and Innovation accelerator, established in 2019 to bring new technology into use faster than traditional procurement routes. In April 2025 the Royal Navy created the Disruptive Capabilities and Technologies Office (DCTO), which formally brought NavyX together with the Office of the Chief Technology Officer and the Navy AI Cell into a single organisation; the NavyX name continues to be used publicly for the trials and technology work it originated.

## Products and solutions

- **XV Patrick Blackett** - the Royal Navy's dedicated experimentation vessel, delivered in 2022, used as a modular testbed for uncrewed, AI and sensor trials.
- **MADFOX** - a 13-metre uncrewed surface vessel used in the Royal Navy's first USV trials, including a demonstration launch of a loitering munition under remote control during NATO exercise REPMUS in 2021.
- **APAC-24** - a remotely controlled and autonomous rigid inflatable boat, evaluated by NavyX including live-firing trials.
- **REPMUS participation** - recurring annual participation in NATO's Robotic Experimentation and Prototyping using Maritime Uncrewed Systems exercise in Portugal, trialling uncrewed surface and subsurface systems, anti-submarine warfare capability, and command-and-control equipment.
- **Navigation and sensor trials** - quantum inertial navigation sensor trials with Imperial College London aboard XV Patrick Blackett, and navigation radar trials using Kelvin Hughes and Saab Giraffe systems.
- **Project VANTAGE** - a tri-service programme trialling maritime one-way strike drones, including the first launch of a strike-capable drone from a Royal Navy ship under way, from XV Patrick Blackett in 2026.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- Delivered through the Royal Navy's relationship with Dstl and the Defence and Security Accelerator (DASA), which fund and support maritime autonomous systems research.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-07-03** - Royal Navy launches a strike-capable Nyan OWE drone from XV Patrick Blackett under way off Dorset, under Project VANTAGE. [source](https://www.navylookout.com/royal-navy-launches-strike-drone-from-xv-patrick-blackett-in-landmark-trial/)
- **2025-09-08** - Royal Navy team prepares for NATO's REPMUS exercise in Portugal, testing uncrewed surface vessels, ocean gliders and command-and-control interfaces. [source](https://www.royalnavy.mod.uk/news/2025/september/08/20250908-nato-autonomous-exercise-in-portugal)
- **2025-04-30** - Royal Navy establishes the Disruptive Capabilities and Technologies Office, bringing NavyX, the Office of the Chief Technology Officer and the Navy AI Cell into a single organisation. [source](https://www.royalnavy.mod.uk/news/2025/april/29/20250430-creation-of-dcto)

## Links

- Website: https://www.royalnavy.mod.uk
- Careers: https://www.royalnavy.mod.uk/careers

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Royal Navy public site (royalnavy.mod.uk), Navy Lookout reporting.

NBOUND provides defence acquisition strategy, SBIR/STTR advisory, and programme management to companies seeking access to the US defence market. The team is composed of former military officers with experience across federal acquisitions, defence programmes and DoD operations.

companies.summary: The National Composites Centre, a High Value Manufacturing Catapult organisation bridging composites research and industrial application across the defence and aerospace sectors.
companies.body:
# NCC

> The National Composites Centre, a High Value Manufacturing Catapult organisation bridging composites research and industrial application across the defence and aerospace sectors.

## What they do

NCC is part of the High Value Manufacturing Catapult and is affiliated with the University of Bristol, having evolved from the National Centre of Excellence in composite technology. Based in Bristol, it translates advanced composites research into industrial impact across the entire product lifecycle, from concept to end-of-life, working with companies of all sizes including defence and aerospace primes and their supply chains. The centre states it has over £250 million invested in its capability and describes itself as a Centre of Excellence for Defence Innovation, supporting UK defence programmes to reduce risk and accelerate the development of advanced materials, including the UK's first carbon fibre development facility.

## Products and solutions

- **Composite manufacturing capability** - automated fibre placement, braiding, ultra-high-rate deposition, ceramic matrix composites and resin impregnation, described as among the world's most advanced automated composites manufacturing facilities.
- **Test and verification** - materials and component testing and verification services supporting the composites product lifecycle.
- **Defence Innovation programme** - dedicated defence-sector engagement supporting MOD and industry partners on advanced materials development.
- **Digital and connectivity infrastructure** - a private industrial 5G network and participation in the UK's first quantum-secure industrial network.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, Airbus AIP103-02-019 aerospace accreditation.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025-12** - NCC announced establishment of the UK's first Large Structures Innovation Centre, to be sited on the Isle of Wight, with Vestas confirmed as launch partner. [source](https://www.compositesworld.com/news/ncc-to-establish-uk-based-large-structures-innovation-centre)
- **2026-02** - Isle of Wight site confirmed for the Large Structures Innovation Centre, with first innovation projects due to begin in 2026 and formal launch planned for 2027. [source](https://www.watermagazine.co.uk/2026/02/04/large-structures-innovation-centre-to-be-located-on-the-isle-of-wight/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.nccuk.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ncc-innovation/

# NCC Group

> A global cyber security and resilience firm serving governments, financial institutions, and defence customers across more than 30 years.

## What they do

NCC Group provides end-to-end cyber security and resilience solutions, with capabilities spanning security consultancy, software security testing, managed detection and response, and counter-terrorism and intelligence services. It serves government and defence customers alongside financial institutions and enterprise clients, with a worldwide team of over 2,400 experts.

## Products and solutions

- **Software security and cyber testing** - Penetration testing, data forensics, software security assessment, and cyber systems integrity services.
- **Attack simulation** - Red team, purple team and gold team exercises.

- **Managed security services** - Ongoing monitoring, detection, and response for critical national infrastructure and defence clients.

companies.body:
# Needwood Engineering Consulting Ltd

> Engineering consultancy developing novel vertical/short take-off and landing (VSTOL) aircraft technology, with granted patents in Europe and the United States.

## What they do

Needwood Engineering Consulting is a small engineering consultancy developing proprietary VSTOL (vertical/short take-off and landing) aircraft propulsion technology, holding European Patent EP 3 914 513 B1 and US Patent US 12,291,331 B2. The granted US patent describes a fixed-wing aircraft with at least one propulsion unit mounted forward of the wing's leading edge, with exhaust flow passing over the wing to generate lift and forward thrust, paired with a further propulsion unit providing thrust in the opposite direction. No flying prototype, demonstrator, or named platform has been publicly documented; the company's public footprint is centred on the patent family and its consultancy trading description.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

Public sources describe a fixed-wing VSTOL propulsion concept at IP/consultancy stage rather than a fielded or flown unmanned platform; the `drones`/`uas` tags have been removed pending confirmation the platform is unmanned.

Neptune North is a technical consultancy providing data engineering, AI, software engineering, and quantitative/economics consultancy to clients in defence, financial services, and alternative investment. It describes two engagement models: augmentation, embedding engineers within a client's existing team, and full consultancy, where Neptune North owns a defined scope of work end-to-end. companies.name: Neros Technologies
companies.summary: US drone manufacturer building tactical, attritable one-way attack drones for Western military forces, with a new UK subsidiary.
companies.body:
# Neros Technologies

> US drone manufacturer building tactical, attritable one-way attack drones for Western military forces, with a new UK subsidiary.

## What they do

Neros makes tactical, attritable, one-way drones intended to give Western military forces an asymmetric advantage. The company says it was founded to onshore the manufacturing of drones critical to a modern arsenal, building first-person-view (FPV) systems and AI-enabled mission software with a secure supply chain free of critical Chinese components. It is headquartered in El Segundo, California, with manufacturing in California and additional offices including a UK subsidiary.

## Products and solutions

- **Archer** - mass-produced FPV drone built for modular payloads and resilient communications, with an NDAA-compliant supply chain; selected for the US Defense Innovation Unit's Blue UAS list.
- **Archer Strike** - kinetic-enabled FPV variant for lethal effects at range in contested environments.
- **Archer Fiber** - fibre-optic-controlled FPV drone, jam-resistant with no radio-frequency signature, launched with partner Kela Technologies and marketed as an NDAA-compliant fibre-optic FPV drone.
- **Crossbow** - tactical ground control station with electronic-warfare resistance at range.
- **Flatbow** - ground control system named alongside Archer in the US Army Purpose-Built Attritable Systems selection.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** Blue UAS listed (US Defense Innovation Unit); NDAA-compliant supply chain.

## Recent news

- **2026-03-03** - Launched a UK subsidiary, Neros Technologies UK Ltd, with up to £10m investment over five years and a Swindon headquarters. [source](https://www.neros.tech/articles/neros-launches-uk-subsidiary-with-up-to-ps10m-investment-into-british-defence)
- **2025-12-11** - Unveiled Archer Fiber, an NDAA-compliant fibre-optic FPV drone, with partner Kela Technologies. [source](https://www.unmannedsystemstechnology.com/2025/12/neros-kela-technologies-unveil-ndaa-compliant-fiber-optic-fpv-drone/)
- **2025-11-07** - Closed a $75M Series B led by Sequoia Capital, taking total raised above $120M. [source](https://www.finsmes.com/2025/11/neros-raises-75m-in-series-b-funding.html)
- **2025-11-06** - US Army selected the Neros Archer FPV drone for the Purpose-Built Attritable Systems (PBAS) programme. [source](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251106525503/en/)
- **2025-11-03** - Secured a multi-million-dollar US Marine Corps contract for Archer Strike FPV drones. [source](https://www.neros.tech/articles/neros-secures-multi-million-marine-corps-contract-for-archer-strike-fpv-drones)

## Links

- Website: https://www.neros.tech/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/neros-tech

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (neros.tech), LinkedIn company page, BusinessWire and FinSMEs funding coverage, Unmanned Systems Technology, Army Technology, and ADS Advance. Headcount not published by the company; the medium band is an estimate from third-party aggregators (PitchBook, Wikipedia) and the firm is scaling rapidly. Founded 2023. Primary HQ El Segundo, California; UK subsidiary based in Swindon.

# Netscout Systems UK Ltd

> UK entity of NETSCOUT, providing network detection and response and real-time network visibility to enterprises, service providers, and government agencies.

## What they do

NETSCOUT protects critical digital infrastructure from cyberattacks and performance disruptions using deep packet inspection at scale technology. The company serves government agencies alongside enterprises and service providers, providing network performance management, service assurance, and threat detection capabilities across cloud, on-premise, and hybrid environments.

## Products and solutions

- **Omnis Cyber Intelligence / Omnis CyberStream** - Network detection and response built for SOC teams, integrating with SIEM, XDR, SOAR and EDR workflows for continuous monitoring.
- **Adaptive Threat Analytics** - Threat investigation and incident response capability aimed at reducing detection-to-response time, launched 2025.
- **Performance management and service assurance** - Visibility and monitoring across network, cloud, and service environments, built on deep packet inspection at scale.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-05-07** - NETSCOUT reported fourth-quarter and full fiscal year 2026 results, with revenue of $859.5m, up from $822.7m, and a return to profitability. [source](https://ir.netscout.com/news/news-details/2026/NETSCOUT-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Fiscal-Year-2026-Financial-Results/default.aspx)
- **2025-07-15** - Launched NETSCOUT Adaptive Threat Analytics, aimed at reducing detection-to-response time for security teams. [source](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2025/07/15/netscout-adaptive-threat-analytics/)

## Links

companies.body:
# Networkology Ltd

> Professional services company delivering networking and cyber capability to defence, government and public sector clients.

## What they do

Networkology provides architecture and engineering services focused on networking and cyber within highly regulated environments, including defence and government. Its offer is structured around three service pillars: networks and communications, data solutions, and performance management. A separate Application Performance Management (APM) division supports end-user experience monitoring, baselining and auditing, troubleshooting, and SLA compliance for the same client base.

## Products and solutions

- **Networks and communications** - Network architecture and engineering delivered through named partner practices including Juniper Networks (including Juniper Mist AI wireless), Allied Telesis, and Teltonika Networks for industrial cellular connectivity.
- **Application Performance Management (APM)** - Proactive monitoring, emulation and testing, traffic management, and digital experience monitoring, built substantially on Splunk Observability and Splunk Enterprise Security.
- **Data solutions** - Data pipeline and observability capability, including named Splunk Edge Hub and Cribl offerings.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (Crown Commercial Service), Digital Outcomes and Specialists (DOS)
- **Accreditations:** ISO 27001:2022, ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015, Cyber Essentials Plus, Splunk Elite Partner, Allied Telesis Platinum Partner

# Newbury Electronics Ltd

> UK PCB manufacturer with over 65 years of experience, serving defence and aerospace clients from facilities in Newbury, Berkshire.

## What they do

Newbury Electronics designs, fabricates and assembles printed circuit boards at its manufacturing plants in Newbury. It holds aerospace and defence quality approvals including AS9100D, EN9100 and JOSCAR registration, and offers contract electronics manufacturing across a broad range of complexity levels.

## Products and solutions

- **Flexible and flexi-rigid PCBs** - Flexible and rigid-flex circuit boards for space-constrained or high-vibration applications.

- **Aluminium-based copper-clad and controlled impedance PCBs** - Metal-core boards for thermal management and boards built to controlled impedance specifications.
- **Box build and mechanical assembly** - Full box build, conformal coating (selective, CNC and automated), laser-cut solder paste stencils, laser micro-machining, in-house X-ray inspection, and CNC de-panelling.

companies.name: Newcastle University
companies.summary: Research-intensive university with defence-relevant expertise in naval architecture, marine engineering, aerospace engineering and intelligent sensing.
companies.body:
# Newcastle University

> Research-intensive university with defence-relevant expertise in naval architecture, marine engineering, aerospace engineering and intelligent sensing.

## What they do

Newcastle University's School of Engineering covers naval architecture and marine engineering, aerospace engineering, electrical and electronic engineering, and geospatial engineering, among other disciplines. The university runs one of the UK's larger dedicated marine technology research groups, with facilities including a cavitation tunnel and propulsion and hydrodynamics laboratories, and states a "Security and Defence" research theme spanning maritime and offshore engineering, aerospace, sensing and communications, autonomous systems, earth observation and geospatial intelligence, advanced materials, and artificial intelligence. It states it does not support research intended solely for direct offensive military activity, and that its endowment does not invest in armament manufacturers.

## Products and solutions

- **Intelligent Sensing Laboratory** - university research facility for sensing and communications research, opened 2016.
- **EPSRC Centre for Doctoral Training in Geospatial Systems** - doctoral training centre run jointly with the University of Nottingham.
- **Academic Centre of Excellence in Cyber Security Research** - designation held since 2013.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** registered supplier on the R-Cloud portal, Accelerated Capability Environment (ACE) and Serapis framework.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; founding member of the North East Regional Defence and Security Cluster (NERDSC); member of the Research Institute in Science of Cyber Security (RISCS); Armed Forces Covenant signatory.
- **Accreditations:** Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering degree accredited by the Royal Institution of Naval Architects (RINA).

## Recent news

- **2026-06-09** - Newcastle University named among 24 UK universities and colleges sharing an £80m Ministry of Defence and Office for Students Strategic Priorities Grant to expand defence-related engineering and computing student places. [source](https://www.gov.uk/government/news)
- **2026-04-29** - Newcastle University joined a university consortium under the UK-Indonesia Maritime Partnership Programme, covering maritime defence, skills and industry cooperation. [source](https://www.ncl.ac.uk)
- **2026-03-17** - Newcastle University joined CHIMES², a £16.7m National ICT Knowledge Centre for next-generation semiconductor design, supporting the UK Semiconductor Strategy. [source](https://www.ncl.ac.uk)

## Links

- Website: https://www.ncl.ac.uk
- Security and defence research: https://www.ncl.ac.uk/research/research-governance/security-defence/
- Marine and offshore engineering: https://www.ncl.ac.uk/engineering/research/marine-offshore-engineering/

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. Newcastle University website (ncl.ac.uk), including its Security and Defence research pages, engineering research pages and ethics toolkit; GOV.UK; North East Regional Defence and Security Cluster (nerdsc.co.uk); Geospatial Systems CDT (geospatialcdt.ac.uk).

companies.name: Nexor Limited
companies.summary: UK cyber security company specialising in secure information exchange and cross-domain solutions.
companies.body:
# Nexor Limited

> UK cyber security company specialising in secure information exchange and cross-domain solutions.

## What they do

Nexor is a privately held UK cyber security company based in Nottingham, providing products and services to safeguard government, defence and critical national infrastructure systems. It specialises in secure information exchange, or cross-domain, solutions that enforce the controlled flow of data between separate security domains, applying data validation, sanitisation and policy enforcement so only authorised, clean information is exchanged. Nexor states more than 30 years of work alongside the UK MOD and NATO; the company traces its origins to X-Tel Services Limited, founded in 1989, later renamed Nexor.

## Products and solutions

- **Nexor GuarDiode** - secure, uni-directional data transfer to safely import and filter data from untrusted networks.
- **Nexor Guardian** - reduces risk of malware attacks and data loss across domains.
- **Nexor Data Diode** - hardware-enforced one-way transfer preventing data leaking back to an untrusted network.
- **Nexor Sentinel** - prevents information loss via email and reduces malware risk.

## Recent news

- **2024-07** - SInfoE, developed by Nexor for the UK MOD, was tested during the Acheron trials integrating Royal Navy, British Army and Royal Air Force units with industry unmanned systems. [source](https://www.nexor.com/blog/enabling-the-future-of-uk-defence)
- **2025** - Nexor highlighted its cross-domain and secure information sharing work in the context of the UK Strategic Defence Review 2025. [source](https://www.nexor.com/blog/inside-the-2025-strategic-defence-review)

## Links

- Website: https://www.nexor.com/
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/nexor

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Nexor website (nexor.com), Nexor LinkedIn company page, Companies House (company 05152465), Nexor blog, Wikipedia (Nexor).

companies.body:
# NI

> Test and measurement technology company providing hardware and software platforms for aerospace, defence and government applications, now part of Emerson.

## What they do

NI (formerly National Instruments) supplies modular instrumentation, data acquisition hardware, and automated test software , including LabVIEW, PXI platforms and software-defined radio to customers in aerospace, defence and government. Its defence-relevant focus areas include radar test, electronic warfare, avionics communication buses and RF signal analysis. Its Electromagnetic Spectrum Operations materials state that its solutions support radar, communications and navigation system testing across radar, EW and SIGINT applications. NI's role is as a supplier of test, prototyping and validation equipment rather than of operational EW or radar effectors.

## Products and solutions

- **PXI Platform** - High-performance modular test platform used for aerospace and defence system validation.
- **LabVIEW** - Graphical programming environment widely used in defence test and measurement workflows.
- **Software Defined Radios (FlexRIO, USRP)** - Reconfigurable RF instruments supporting radar and EW test applications; USRP devices are marketed for real-time direction finding and spectrum monitoring.
- **VeriStand** - Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test development environment.
- **Radar and EW system test** - dedicated test solutions for radar warning receivers, jammers and the wider spectrum of electronic warfare systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.ni.com

companies.name: NIACE
companies.summary: Northern Ireland Advanced Composites and Engineering Centre; a technology hub for research and development in advanced materials and engineering, serving aerospace, automotive and defence sectors.
companies.body:
# NIACE

> Northern Ireland Advanced Composites and Engineering Centre; a technology hub for research and development in advanced materials and engineering, serving aerospace, automotive and defence sectors.

## What they do

NIACE was a purpose-built R&D centre at Belfast International Airport, co-owned and operated by Queen's University Belfast and Ulster University, providing collaborative research and technology development in advanced composites and engineering materials. It opened in 2012 with funding from BIS, Invest Northern Ireland and Bombardier Aerospace, and operated as a shared hub for member companies across aerospace, automotive and defence, offering access to specialist facilities and expertise not typically available to individual SMEs. In October 2021 it was re-launched in partnership with the National Composites Centre, giving members access to the UK-wide High Value Manufacturing Catapult network, with support from Invest Northern Ireland, Spirit AeroSystems and BEIS.

**Note on current status.** The website previously recorded for NIACE (niace-centre.org.uk) no longer resolves to the organisation; it now redirects to unrelated third-party content. NIACE's activities appear to have been superseded by the Advanced Manufacturing Innovation Centre (AMIC), a Belfast Region City Deal project led by Queen's University Belfast, whose "Factory of the Future" facility at Newtownabbey became operational in April 2026. This entry is retained for historical record; the website field has been left as previously recorded rather than substituted, and should be corrected or the page archived once Matt confirms the successor arrangement.

## Products and solutions

- **Composites research and development facilities** - collaborative office space, a composites research laboratory and materials analysis labs for member companies.
- **Capability areas publicly stated** - joining of composites, reusable bagging materials, recycling of high-performance thermoplastics and thermosets, filament winding, sensor integration into composite structures, and low-cost tooling development.
- **Testing and prototyping** - test matrix definition and material/component testing, plus FDM 3D-printing based rapid prototyping and low-use tooling.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-04** - The Advanced Manufacturing Innovation Centre (AMIC), the successor project associated with NIACE's partner institutions, opened its "Factory of the Future" facility at Newtownabbey. [source](https://www.themanufacturer.com/articles/construction-starts-on-factory-of-the-future-to-reinvigorate-northern-irelands-industrial-potential/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.niace-centre.org.uk (no longer resolves to the organisation; see note above)

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-07-05. ADS Group members directory (snapshot, 2026-05-15). Historical NIACE facility and re-launch details from ulster.ac.uk and nccuk.com; AMIC successor details from we-are-amic.com, belfastregioncitydeal.co.uk and themanufacturer.com.

# Nicholsons Sealing Technologies Limited

> Manufacturer of precision resilient metal seals, gaskets and ancillary components for extreme-environment applications, with NADCAP and aerospace quality accreditations.

## What they do

Nicholsons Sealing Technologies designs and manufactures resilient, high-temperature metal seals and gaskets, headquartered in Stanley, County Durham, with design, sales and manufacturing sites across Europe and the USA. It offers a complete engineering service from design and prototyping through to full-volume production, covering standard, optimised and bespoke sealing solutions. Its seals handle media ranging from helium gas to heavy oil, across environments from cryogenic to high temperature and vacuum to high pressure. Sectors served, as stated on its own site, include aerospace, automotive, turbochargers, oil and gas, power generation, nuclear and medical applications. In-house capabilities include machining, rolling, forming, heat treatment and chemical processing.

## Products and solutions

- **Corruseals and Reflexseals** - crush-type gaskets using a triangular sealing corrugation, in single or multi-ply construction.
- **Corruplus** - high-temperature, low-load sealing solution for small cavities.
- **Flexible Metal Seals, C-Seals, Boss Seals, E-Seals** - standard metal seal ranges for extreme-environment sealing.
- **Special Products range** - machined seals, formed seals, pipe-end seals, bellows, complex assemblies, shims, baffles, clamps and clips.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2015, AS9100D, IATF 16949:2016, ISO 14001:2015, ISO 45001:2018, Nadcap (Chemical Processing, Non-conventional Machining, Welding).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

> Glasgow-based hydraulic cylinder and ram repair and manufacturing company, serving defence and industrial clients from its Hillington facility.

## What they do

Nicol & Andrew (Hydraulics) Ltd operates from Hillington Industrial Estate, Glasgow, tracing its roots to a parent company founded in 1952. It provides hydraulic cylinder and ram repair, overhaul and manufacture, with in-house machining and stocks of chrome-plated rod material. All cylinders are pressure tested and repairs carry a warranty. companies.body:
# Nightball Technologies Ltd

> Independent cyber security specialist delivering zero-trust and cyber-resilient solutions for defence and critical operational environments.

## What they do

Nightball, based in Basingstoke, provides end-to-end digital solutions focused on cyber resilience for high-risk and critical operational environments. The company states that cyber problems cannot be resolved by technology alone, and that its approach covers people, process, environment and technology. Its stated areas of expertise include privileged access management, adopting the Principle of Least Privilege for enterprise environments; classified and high-threat environment solutions, including public sector classified and multi-national work; high-performance solutions for high-density, scalable workloads supporting AI/ML and end-user compute; system design and integration of classified secure systems; zero-trust architecture across public and private cloud, including separation and continuous authentication; and rapid prototyping of new concepts through its Nightball Lab.

## Products and solutions

- **CRIW (Cyber Resilient Integrated Workspace)** - trademarked, patent-pending zero-trust, modular system providing cyber resilient distributed and disconnected cloud operating environments, positioned for critical national infrastructure.
- **CLVR** - joint venture providing secure, platform-agnostic distributed sensing capabilities, performing multi-modal detection, identification and tracking with edge analytics from distributed sensors.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 27001:2022, Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus
